```text
## HOMEPAGE & LAYOUT STRUCTURE REPORT

### **Current Homepage (about.md) — Visible Sections & Order**

The homepage (`_pages/about.md` → `_layouts/about.liquid` → `_layouts/default.liquid`) displays sections in this sequence:

1. **Title bar** — "ZHENGYANG WAN" in bold + subtitle "Ph.D. student at The University of Wisconsin-Madison CEE" with red highlight (line 5-6)
2. **Social icons** — GitHub, Google Scholar, ORCID, LinkedIn, etc. (about.liquid:18-21, social.liquid:1-133)
3. **Profile card** — Right-aligned, non-circular photo (DSCF1344.webp, about.md:8-13, about.liquid:30-50)
4. **Bio text** — Dense multi-paragraph prose (~250 words) with inline institution logos (about.md:19-31)
5. **Horizontal divider** (about.liquid:56)
6. **Recent News** — Table with date + inline/linked items, max 7 entries (about.liquid:59-64, news.liquid:1-34)
7. **Horizontal divider** (about.liquid:80)
8. **Selected Publications** — BibTeX filtered by `selected={true}` (3 papers shown, about.liquid:83-88, selected_papers.liquid:1-3)
9. **Visitor count heatmap** — ClusterMaps widget (about.liquid:96-100)

No hero image, no featured projects strip, no timeline, no testimonials.

---

### **Navigation Structure**

**Header/Navbar** (`_includes/header.liquid:1-150`, fixed-top per `_config.yml:49`):
- Logo links to home only on non-home pages (header.liquid:5-22)
- Right-aligned navbar items:
  - **About** (home, always visible)
  - **Publications** (nav_order: 2, _pages/publications.md:6-7)
  - **Projects** (nav_order: 3, _pages/projects.md:5-6)
  - Other pages: CV, News, Blog, Repositories, Teaching, Profiles (inferred from _pages glob)
  - **Dropdown support** (header.liquid:63-99) — unused currently
  - **Search toggle** (Ctrl+K, header.liquid:122-126, enabled by _config.yml:51)
  - **Dark/Light theme toggle** (header.liquid:130-136, enabled by _config.yml:438)
  - **Progress bar** (header.liquid:144-148, enabled by _config.yml:442)

No navbar social icons (enable_navbar_social: false, _config.yml:439).

---

### **Footer** (`_includes/footer.liquid:1-35`)

Fixed bottom (sticky-bottom-footer class). Contains:
- Copyright year + name
- Attribution: "Powered by Jekyll with al-folio theme. Hosted by GitHub Pages."
- Last updated timestamp (if enabled, _config.yml:21)
- No social links, no newsletter signup

---

### **Includes & Data Sources**

| File | Purpose | Notes |
|------|---------|-------|
| `_includes/social.liquid` | Renders icon links to ~40 platforms (GitHub, Google Scholar, ORCID, LinkedIn, WeChat, etc.) | Conditional on `_config.yml` values; email masked |
| `_includes/news.liquid` | Renders `_news/*.md` entries as a scrollable table (max-height: 60vw if scrollable enabled) | Limit: 7 items; date + title/inline content |
| `_includes/selected_papers.liquid` | Renders BibTeX entries with `selected={true}` via jekyll-scholar plugin | Line 2: `{% bibliography --query @*[selected=true]* %}` |
| `_includes/figure.liquid` | Lazy-loads profile image with WebP responsive sizing | Cache-bust, z-depth-1 shadow, rounded corners |

---

### **Config Highlights** (`_config.yml`)

**Identity:**
- Name: Zhengyang Wan (first_name, last_name; title: blank → full name used)
- Email: zhengyang.wan@wisc.edu
- Description: "Personal academic website..."
- Keywords: "autonomous driving, intelligent transportation, vision-language model"

**Features Enabled/Disabled:**
- `announcements.enabled: true` — Shows news section with 7-item limit, scrollable if >5
- `latest_posts.enabled: true` (commented out in about.liquid:66-76)
- `enable_darkmode: true` — Light/dark toggle in navbar
- `enable_search: true` — Ctrl+K search modal
- `navbar_fixed: true` — Sticky header
- `footer_fixed: true` — Fixed bottom footer
- `enable_publication_badges: true` — Altmetric, Dimensions, Google Scholar badges on papers
- `enable_publication_thumbnails: true` — Paper preview images
- `enable_math: true` — MathJax enabled
- `enable_medium_zoom: true` — Click-to-zoom images
- `enable_progressbar: true` — Scroll position bar
- `repo_trophies.enabled: true` — GitHub profile trophies (unused on homepage)

**Max content width:** 930px (`max_width: 930px`)

**Bibliography:** APA style, grouped by year (descending), with custom badges/links (lines 314-346).

---

### **News & Bibliography Content Density**

**News (_news/*.md):** 11 items spanning 2018–2025. Example:
- announcement_1.md: "I began pursuing my Bachelor's degree at Tongji University.🧐" (inline, no link)
- announcement_11 and others: Likely mix of inline text + linked items

**Bibliography (papers.bib top 60 lines):** 
- 3 papers shown as "selected"
- Titles: Sim2Real-AD, DriveVLM-RL, Sky-Drive (all 2025–2026, arXiv/JICV)
- Rich metadata: arxiv ID, preview image, abstract, GitHub repo, video link, annotation
- Publication badges enabled (Altmetric, Dimensions, Google Scholar)

**Profile image:** DSCF1344.webp (sunset beach photo, casual, professional), right-aligned, responsive sizing with Sass variables.

---

### **Non-Standard Customizations vs Stock al-folio**

1. **Inline logos in bio** — Custom inline `<img>` tags for UW-Madison, Sky-Lab, Tongji, Bucky icons (about.md:19-31)
2. **Red highlight text** — `.highlight-text` class; custom color #c5051c (about.md:5, _layout.scss:41-44)
3. **Visitor heatmap** — ClusterMaps widget added at bottom of about.liquid (lines 96-100)
4. **Custom profile location** — "Chicago, US, 2025" in more_info block (about.md:12-13)
5. **Newsletter disabled** — (`newsletter.enabled: false`, _config.yml:183)
6. **Blog posts section commented out** — Latest_posts include removed from about.liquid (lines 66-76)

**No custom plugins** (uses standard al-folio set: jekyll-scholar, jekyll-feed, jekyll-archives, jemoji, etc.).

---

### **Visual Weaknesses & Missing Patterns**

1. **No hero section** — Homepage starts with plain title; no eye-catching banner, background image, or tagline callout
2. **Dense text-wall bio** — 31 lines of paragraph text before any visual breaks; very academic/verbose
3. **Minimal visual hierarchy** — Profile card is only visual relief; rest is headings + text tables
4. **Missing CTAs** — No "Recent Work" button, "Download CV" link, or "Contact Me" button above fold
5. **No featured projects strip** — Only three publications shown; projects page is separate
6. **Inactive latest_posts section** — Blog enabled but hidden on homepage
7. **No timeline** — Education/career milestones listed inline rather than as interactive timeline
8. **Static visitor counter** — Heatmap at bottom is ornamental, not integrated
9. **No testimonials or achievements** — No quote section, no badge/medal summary (despite having GitHub trophies enabled)
10. **Missing "News" context** — News items are bare dates + titles; no category/tag filters
11. **No modern patterns** — No gradient overlays, no card shadows on main sections, minimal use of color (only red subtitle + default blue links)

---

### **Key File References**

| Path | Key Lines |
|------|-----------|
| `_pages/about.md` | 1–31 (front matter + bio content) |
| `_layouts/about.liquid` | 1–102 (profile + news + publications assembly) |
| `_layouts/default.liquid` | 1–77 (global chrome: header, container, footer, scripts) |
| `_includes/header.liquid` | 1–150 (navbar with dropdowns, search, dark mode) |
| `_includes/footer.liquid` | 1–35 (copyright, attribution, timestamp) |
| `_includes/news.liquid` | 1–34 (news table rendering) |
| `_includes/selected_papers.liquid` | 1–3 (BibTeX filter) |
| `_includes/social.liquid` | 1–133 (40+ social icon links) |
| `_config.yml` | 5–652 (site config, feature toggles, bibliography) |
| `assets/css/main.scss` | 1–26 (Sass import chain) |
| `_sass/_layout.scss` | 1–59 (profile + highlight-text styling) |

---

**Summary:** Your site is a functional, feature-rich academic portfolio using al-folio defaults with light customization (inline logos, red subtitle, visitor widget). It's **information-dense but visually flat**—ideal for a research-focused audience but lacking modern visual appeal (hero, gradients, clear hierarchy, featured CTAs). A redesign should balance academic credibility with contemporary UX patterns (hero with tagline, visual card sections, timeline, project highlights, contact CTA).
```

```text
304	    tag: "/blog/tag/:name/"
305	    category: "/blog/category/:name/"
306	
307	display_tags: ["formatting", "images", "links", "math", "code", "blockquotes"] # these tags will be displayed on the front page of your blog
308	display_categories: ["external-services"] # these categories will be displayed on the front page of your blog
309	
310	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
311	# Jekyll Scholar
312	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
313	
314	scholar:
315	  last_name: [Wan]
316	  first_name: [Zhengyang]
317	
318	  style: apa
319	  locale: en
320	
321	  source: /_bibliography/
322	  bibliography: papers.bib
323	  bibliography_template: bib
324	  # Note: if you have latex math in your bibtex, the latex filter
325	  # preprocessing may conflict with MathJAX if the latter is enabled.
326	  # See https://github.com/alshedivat/al-folio/issues/357.
327	  bibtex_filters: [latex, smallcaps, superscript]
328	
329	  replace_strings: true
330	  join_strings: true
331	
332	  details_dir: bibliography
333	  details_link: Details
334	
335	  query: "@*"
336	  group_by: year
337	  group_order: descending
338	
339	# Display different badges withs stats for your publications
340	# Customize badge behavior in _layouts/bib.liquid
341	enable_publication_badges:
342	  altmetric: true # Altmetric badge (Customization options: https://badge-docs.altmetric.com/index.html)
343	  dimensions: true # Dimensions badge (Customization options: https://badge.dimensions.ai/)
344	  google_scholar: true # Google Scholar badge (https://scholar.google.com/intl/en/scholar/citations.html)
345	  github_stars: true # GitHub stars badge for papers with associated repos
346	  inspirehep: true # Inspire HEP badge (https://help.inspirehep.net/knowledge-base/citation-metrics/)
347	
348	# Filter out certain bibtex entry keywords used internally from the bib output
349	filtered_bibtex_keywords:
350	  [
351	    abbr,
352	    abstract,
353	    additional_info,
354	    altmetric,
355	    annotation,
356	    arxiv,
357	    award,
358	    award_name,
359	    bibtex_show,
360	    blog,
361	    code,
362	    github_repo,
363	    google_scholar_id,
364	    html,
365	    inspirehep_id,
366	    pdf,
367	    poster,
368	    preview,
369	    selected,
370	    slides,
371	    supp,
372	    video,
373	    website,
374	  ]
375	
376	# Maximum number of authors to be shown for each publication (more authors are visible on click)
377	max_author_limit: 3 # leave blank to always show all authors
378	more_authors_animation_delay: 10 # more authors are revealed on click using animation; smaller delay means faster animation
379	
380	# Enables publication thumbnails. If disabled, none of the publications will display thumbnails, even if specified in the bib entry.
381	enable_publication_thumbnails: true
382	
383	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
384	# Jekyll Link Attributes
385	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
386	
387	# These are the defaults
388	external_links:
389	  enabled: true
390	  rel: external nofollow noopener
391	  target: _blank
392	  exclude:
393	
394	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
395	# Responsive WebP Images
396	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
397	
398	# MAKE SURE imagemagick is installed and on your PATH before enabling imagemagick. In a terminal, run:
399	#   convert -version
400	imagemagick:
401	  enabled: true # enables responsive images for your site (recommended, see https://github.com/alshedivat/al-folio/issues/537)
402	  widths:
403	    - 480
404	    - 800
405	    - 1400
406	  input_directories:
407	    - assets/img/
408	  input_formats:
409	    - ".jpg"
410	    - ".jpeg"
411	    - ".png"
412	    - ".tiff"
413	    - ".gif"
414	  output_formats:
415	    webp: "-quality 85"
416	
417	# Lazy loading images
418	#   If you enable lazy loading, all images will add the loading="lazy" attribute.
419	#   This will make your site load faster, but it may not be supported in all browsers.
420	#   You can also set loading="" to other values for specific images to override the default behavior.
421	#   Options: "auto", "eager", "lazy"
422	#   See https://web.dev/browser-level-image-lazy-loading/ for more information.
423	lazy_loading_images: true # enables lazy loading of images (recommended)
424	
425	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
426	# Optional Features
427	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
428	
429	enable_google_analytics: false # enables google analytics
430	enable_cronitor_analytics: false # enables cronitor RUM analytics
431	enable_pirsch_analytics: false # enables Pirsch analytics (https://pirsch.io/)
432	enable_openpanel_analytics: false # enables Openpanel analytics (https://openpanel.dev/)
433	enable_google_verification: false # enables google site verification
434	enable_bing_verification: false # enables bing site verification
435	enable_masonry: true # enables automatic project cards arrangement
436	enable_math: true # enables math typesetting (uses MathJax)
437	enable_tooltips: false # enables automatic tooltip links generated for each section titles on pages and posts
438	enable_darkmode: true # enables switching between light/dark modes
439	enable_navbar_social: false # enables displaying social links in the navbar on the about page
440	enable_project_categories: true # enables categorization of projects into multiple categories
441	enable_medium_zoom: true # enables image zoom feature (as on medium.com)
442	enable_progressbar: true # enables a horizontal progress bar linked to the vertical scroll position
443	enable_video_embedding: false # enables video embedding for bibtex entries. If false, the button opens the video link in a new window.
444
```

 `./\_pages\about.md`

```text
1	---
2	layout: about
3	title: About
4	permalink: /
5	subtitle: <span class="highlight-text"> Ph.D. student </span>at The University of Wisconsin-Madison CEE
6	
7	
8	profile:
9	  align: right
10	  image: DSCF1344.webp
11	  image_circular: false # crops the image to make it circular
12	  more_info: >
13	    <p style="margin-left: 35px;">Chicago, US, 2025</p>
14	
15	news: true # includes a list of news items
16	selected_papers: true # includes a list of papers marked as "selected={true}"
17	social: true # includes social icons at the bottom of the page
18	---
19	Hello there!🤺
20	
21	I am a first-year **Ph.D. student** in the Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering at the <img src="../assets/img/uwmadison.png" width="14" height="22" style="vertical-align: middle; margin: 0 2px;"/>[University of Wisconsin-Madison](https://www.wisc.edu/) (UW-Madison). I joined <img src="../assets/img/skylab.png" width="33" height="22" style="vertical-align: middle; margin: 0 2px;"/>[Sky-Lab](https://sky-lab-uw.github.io/) at UW-Madison in Fall 2025 under the guidance of Dr.[Sikai (Sky) Chen](https://sky-lab-uw.github.io/people/). On Wisconsin!<img src="../assets/img/bucky.png" width="15" height="22" style="vertical-align: middle; margin: 0 2px;"/>
22	
23	Before joining UW-Madison, I obtained my M.S. in Transportation Engineering at [College of Transportation](https://tjjt.tongji.edu.cn/), <img src="../assets/img/tongji.png" width="22" height="22" style="vertical-align: middle; margin: 0 2px;"/>Tongji University in 2025, and my B.E. in Vehicle Engineering at [Institute of Rail Transit](https://railway.tongji.edu.cn/main.htm), Tongji University in 2022. I am very fortunate and honored to be advised by Prof. [Hechao Zhou](https://tjjt.tongji.edu.cn/info/2943/10933.htm) and Prof. [Jimin Zhang](https://railway.tongji.edu.cn/c5/e6/c4609a50662/page.htm) during my study at Tongji University. 🫶
24	
25	As for now, I am working to accelerate the development of future intelligent transportation technologies. 🧐 Specifically, I focus on:
26	
27	- **Autonomous driving**
28	- **Vision-Language-Action model**
29	- **Intelligent transportation systems**
30	
31	An equally important dimension of my work is to validate autonomous driving related methods through **real‑vehicle testing**: I have contributed to the development of <img src="../assets/img/Sky-Drive.png" width="25" height="25" style="vertical-align: middle; margin: 0 2px;"/>[SkyDrive](https://github.com/BillWan-zzzyyy/Sky-Drive), an open source multi-agent platform for future transportation system and autonomous driving by Sky-Lab.
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
## Visual Design System Report: BillWan-zzzyyy.github.io

### Partial Structure (`_sass/` directory)

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `_variables.scss` | Color tokens, spacing constants, FontAwesome/Tabler paths |
| `_themes.scss` | CSS variables for light/dark mode theming |
| `_base.scss` | Core typography, card styling, navbar, publications, projects, components |
| `_layout.scss` | Container layout, max-width (930px), profile, highlight text |
| `_distill.scss` | Blog-specific overrides for d-article, d-byline elements |
| `_cv.scss` | CV/resume styling: timeline badges, institution tables, buttons |
| `_tabs.scss` | Tab navigation styling |
| `_typograms.scss` | Diagram/typogram SVG styling |

**Entry point:** `assets/css/main.scss` (lines 9-24) imports in order: variables → themes → layout → base → distill → cv → tabs → typograms → FontAwesome + Tabler icons.

---

### Color Palette

**Light Mode (`:root`)** — `_themes.scss:7-77`
- **Theme Color:** `#c5050c` (red) — used for links, active states, badges
- **Hover Color:** `#0099cc` (bright cyan)
- **Background:** `#ffffff` (white)
- **Text:** `#000000` (black)
- **Footer:** `#1c1c1d` (dark grey) bg, light grey text

- **Divider:** `rgba(0,0,0,0.1)`

**Dark Mode (`html[data-theme="dark"]`)** — `_themes.scss:80-123`
- **Theme Color:** `#2698ba` (cyan)
- **Hover Color:** `#2698ba` (cyan)

- **Text:** light grey
- **Card Background:** `#212529`
- **Code Background:** `#2c3237`
- **Divider:** `#424246`

**Accent Colors** (defined in `_variables.scss:9-27`):
- Red: `#ff3636` / `#b71c1c`; Custom: `#c5050c`
- Blue: `#0076df` / `#00369f`; Custom: `#0099cc`
- Cyan: `#2698ba`, Light: `color.adjust($cyan, +25% lightness)`
- Green: `#00ab37`, Light: `#ddffdd`, Bright: `#11d68b`
- Purple: `#b509ac`, Light: `color.adjust(+25% lightness)`
- Orange: `#f29105`, Light: `#FFFFCC`

**Status/Block Colors** — `_themes.scss:32-45`:

- **Warnings:** `#e7c000` border, `#fff8d8` bg, `#6b5900` text
- **Danger:** `#c00` border, `#ffe0e0` bg, `#600` text

---

### Typography

**Font Stack** — `_base.scss:7-17`
- **Body/Serif:** `'Source Serif 4', ui-serif, Georgia, 'Times New Roman', serif`
- **Headings (h1-h6):** `'Inter', -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif`
- **Code/Mono:** `'JetBrains Mono', SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Monaco, Consolas, 'Liberation Mono', monospace`

**Font Sizes** (sampling from codebase):
- **h6:** `1.5rem` + 6px underline (`_base.scss:39-57`)
- **Blog title (h1):** `5rem` (`_base.scss:558`)
- **Publication title:** `1.1rem` (`_base.scss:765`)
- **Post meta/tags:** `0.875rem` (`_base.scss:600, 606`)
- **Code (inline):** `3px padding`, border-radius `3px` (`_base.scss:904-909`)
- **Pre (code block):** `6px 12px padding`, border-radius `6px` (`_base.scss:889-902`)

**Font weights:** 400, 500, 600, 700 (from Google Fonts load).

---

### Layout Constants

- **Max Content Width:** `930px` (`_config.yml:56`, `main.scss:7`)

- **Footer:** 70px padding-bottom (body: `_layout.scss:6`)
- **Card Padding:** `1.25rem` (`_base.scss:219`)
- **Card Border Radius:** Not explicitly set (Bootstrap default)
- **Border Radius (small):** `6px` (newsletter input, code blocks; `_base.scss:1223, 892`)
- **Border Radius (tiny):** `3px` (inline code; `_base.scss:907`)

### Reusable Components & Styles

| Component | File:Lines | Notes |

| **Tabs** | `_tabs.scss:1-49` | `.tab > li > a`: 9px vertical padding, 12px font-size, uppercase, 2px bottom border (theme color when active) |

| **Repository** | `_base.scss:542-546` | `.repo`: max-width 50% on md+ screens |
| **Button** | `_base.scss:801-818` (pub links), `_cv.scss:13-34` | 1px border, inherit text color, hover → theme color |
| **Newsletter Form** | `_base.scss:1204-1272` | Input: 8px 12px padding, 6px border-radius; Button: 9px 17px, theme color bg |
| **Progress Bar** | `_base.scss:945-998` | Fixed top (56px), height 5px, theme color |

---

### Dark Mode Implementation

**Status:** Fully implemented, enabled by default (`_config.yml:438`).

**Toggle Mechanism:**
- CSS class: `html[data-theme="dark"]` or `html[data-theme-setting="dark/light"]`
- Toggle buttons: `#light-toggle-system`, `#light-toggle-dark`, `#light-toggle-light` (`_themes.scss:48-160`)
- All colors use CSS variables (`--global-*`), switched via data-theme attribute

**Images:** `.repo-img-light` / `.repo-img-dark` shown/hidden via `data-theme` (`_themes.scss:62-67, 117-122`)

---

### Custom CSS Beyond Main Entry

- **Bootstrap 4.6.0** (minified, `assets/css/bootstrap.min.css`)
- **Material Design Bootstrap 4.20.0** (minified, `assets/css/mdb.min.css`)
- **Academicons** (minified, `assets/css/academicons.min.css`)
- **Highlight.js** (light/dark CSS, `assets/css/jekyll-pygments-themes-*.css`)
- **Jupyter CSS** (3 variants, `assets/css/jupyter*.css`)
- **Bootstrap-TOC** (minified, `assets/css/bootstrap-toc.min.css`)

No custom CSS beyond the main SCSS entry.

---

### Icon System

- **FontAwesome 6.x:** brands, solid, regular weights (`main.scss:18-21`)
- **Tabler Icons:** 3 variants (filled, outline, default) (`main.scss:22-23`)
- **Academicons:** institutional/academic badges
- **Material Icons:** Google Fonts loaded (`_config.yml:494`)

Icons sized variably: social (1.7rem in navbar), CV (0.8-0.95rem), etc.

---

### Visual Assessment: Stock vs. Customized

**Overall Status:** **Lightly Customized Stock al-folio** (80% stock, 20% custom).

**Stock Elements:**
- Bootstrap 4 foundation with MDB
- Standard al-folio card layout, publication styling
- Typical serif (Source Serif 4) + sans (Inter) + mono (JetBrains) stack
- Dark mode toggle via CSS variables (al-folio standard)

**Custom Touches:**
- **Color tweak:** Red theme (`#c5050c`) instead of al-folio's default blue
- **H6 underline:** Custom h6 styling with 60px, 6px underline (`_base.scss:38-57`)
- **Highlight text:** Red color class (`.highlight-text: #c5051c`) for post content
- **Visitor tracking:** ClusterMaps embed in footer (`about.liquid:96-99`)
- **Chinese comments:** Inline Chinese in code (e.g., `_base.scss:41-44`)

**Visually Dated/Underdeveloped:**
1. **TODOs unfilled:** `_layout.scss` has TODO comments for publications/projects redefinition (lines 52, 58)
2. **Minimal shadows:** No `box-shadow` usage; relies on 1px borders and transparency
3. **Limited spacing scale:** Mostly `0.5rem`, `1rem`, `1.5rem`, `2rem` increments (no 8px system)
4. **Card styling:** No rounded corners explicitly set (Bootstrap default ~0.25rem)
5. **Hover effects:** Limited to color change + underline; no lift, scale, or shadow transitions
6. **Typography hierarchy:** Slight but not bold—body serif, headings sans-serif, but limited weight variation
7. **Component variety:** Publication/project cards feel generic; no custom illustrations, badges, or tags
8. **Spacing consistency:** Footer, navbar, content all serviceable but not deliberately designed

**File:Line Touchpoints for Redesign:**
- Theme colors: `_themes.scss:12, 85` (light/dark theme-color)
- Typography: `_base.scss:8, 12, 16` (font families)
- Card styling: `_base.scss:206-221`
- Publication styling: `_base.scss:698-882` (large, needs refactor)
- Dark mode: `_themes.scss:1-123` (CSS variables root)
- Navbar: `_base.scss:283-363`
- Layout max-width: `main.scss:7`

---

**Conclusion:** This is a **functional academic website** using al-folio's solid foundation with minor theming. It prioritizes content over visual polish. A redesign should focus on:
1. Introducing consistent shadow/depth
2. Expanding component library (project/news cards need visual distinction)
3. Tightening spacing grid
4. Adding micro-interactions (hover states, transitions)
5. Refactoring publication section (currently 184 lines in one file)
```

```text
24	  title={DriveVLM-RL: Neuroscience-Inspired Reinforcement Learning with Vision-Language Models for Safe and Deployable Autonomous Driving},
25	  author={Huang, Zilin and Sheng, Zihao and Wan, Zhengyang and Qu, Yansong and You, Junwei and Jiang, Sicong and Chen✉, Sikai},
26	  journal={arXiv preprint arXiv:2603.18315},

33	  preview={drivevlmrl.webp},
34	  abstract={Ensuring safe decision-making in autonomous vehicles remains a fundamental challenge despite rapid advances in end-to-end learning approaches. Traditional reinforcement learning (RL) methods rely on manually engineered rewards or sparse collision signals, which fail to capture the rich contextual understanding required for safe driving and make unsafe exploration unavoidable in real-world settings. Recent vision-language models (VLMs) offer promising semantic understanding capabilities; however, their high inference latency and susceptibility to hallucination hinder direct application to real-time vehicle control. To address these limitations, this paper proposes DriveVLM-RL, a neuroscience-inspired framework that integrates VLMs into RL through a dual-pathway architecture for safe and deployable autonomous driving. The framework decomposes semantic reward learning into a Static Pathway for continuous spatial safety assessment using CLIP-based contrasting language goals, and a Dynamic Pathway for attention-gated multi-frame semantic risk reasoning using a lightweight detector and a large VLM. A hierarchical reward synthesis mechanism fuses semantic signals with vehicle states, while an asynchronous training pipeline decouples expensive VLM inference from environment interaction. All VLM components are used only during offline training and are removed at deployment, ensuring real-time feasibility. Experiments in the CARLA simulator show significant improvements in collision avoidance, task success, and generalization across diverse traffic scenarios, including strong robustness under settings without explicit collision penalties. These results demonstrate that DriveVLM-RL provides a practical paradigm for integrating foundation models into autonomous driving without compromising real-time feasibility.},
35	  annotation={* Equal Contribution<br>✉ Corresponding Author},
36	  selected={true}
37	}

41	  title={Sky-Drive: A Distributed Multi-Agent Simulation Platform for Socially-Aware and Human-AI Collaborative Future Transportation},
42	  author={Huang*, Zilin and Sheng*, Zihao and Wan*, Zhengyang and Qu, Yansong and Luo, Yuhao and Wang, Boyue and Li, Pei and Chen, Yen-Jung and Chen, Jiancong and Long, Keke and Meng, Jiayi and Leng, Yue and Chen✉, Sikai},
43	  journal={Journal of Intelligent and Connected Vehicles},
44	  year={2025},
45	  bibtex_show={true},  
46	  abbr={JICV},
47	  priority={1},
48	  year={2025},
49	  abstract={Recent advances in autonomous system simulation platforms have significantly enhanced the safe and scalable testing of driving policies. However, existing simulators do not yet fully meet the needs of future transportation research, particularly in modeling socially-aware driving agents and enabling effective human-AI collaboration. This paper introduces Sky-Drive, a novel distributed multi-agent simulation platform that addresses these limitations through four key innovations: (a) a distributed architecture for synchronized simulation across multiple terminals; (b) a multi-modal human-in-the-loop framework integrating diverse sensors to collect rich behavioral data; (c) a human-AI collaboration mechanism supporting continuous and adaptive knowledge exchange; and (d) a digital twin (DT) framework for constructing high-fidelity virtual replicas of real-world transportation environments. Sky-Drive supports diverse applications such as autonomous vehicle (AV)-vulnerable road user (VRU) interaction modeling, human-in-the-loop training, socially-aware reinforcement learning, personalized driving policy, and customized scenario generation. Future extensions will incorporate foundation models for context-aware decision support and hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) testing for real-world validation. By bridging scenario generation, data collection, algorithm training, and hardware integration, Sky-Drive has the potential to become a foundational platform for the next generation of socially-aware and human-centered autonomous transportation researc},
50	  html={https://sky-lab-uw.github.io/Sky-Drive-website/},
```

```text
1	---
2	---
3	<div class="row">
4	  {% if site.enable_publication_thumbnails %}
5	    <div class="col col-sm-3 abbr">
6	      {%- if entry.abbr -%}
7	        {% assign badge_color = nil %}
8	        {% if entry.priority == "0" %}
9	          {% assign badge_color = "#B8508A" %}
10	        {% elsif entry.priority == "1" %}
11	          {% assign badge_color = "#96682A" %}
12	        {% elsif entry.priority == "2" %}
13	          {% assign badge_color = "#4A708A" %}
14	        {% elsif entry.priority == "3" %}
15	          {% assign badge_color = "#8C7050" %}
16	        {% elsif site.data.venues[entry.abbr].color != blank %}
17	          {% assign badge_color = site.data.venues[entry.abbr].color %}
18	        {% endif %}
19	        <abbr
20	          class="badge rounded w-100"
21	          {% if badge_color %}style="background-color:{{ badge_color }}; color: white"{% else %}style="color: white"{% endif %}
22	        >
23	          {% if site.data.venues[entry.abbr].url %}
24	            <a href="{{ site.data.venues[entry.abbr].url }}" style="color: white">{{ entry.abbr }}</a>
25	          {% else %}
26	            <div style="color: white">{{- entry.abbr -}}</div>
27	          {% endif %}
28	        </abbr>
29	      {% endif %}
30	      {% if entry.preview %}
31	        {% if entry.preview contains '://' %}
32	          <img class="preview z-depth-1 rounded" src="{{ entry.preview }}">
33	        {% else %}
34	          {% assign entry_path = entry.preview | prepend: '/assets/img/publication_preview/' %}
35	          {%
36	            include figure.liquid
37	            loading="eager"
38	            path=entry_path
39	            sizes = "280px"
40	            class="preview z-depth-1 rounded"
41	            zoomable=true
42	            alt=entry.preview
43	          %}
44	        {% endif %}
45	      {% endif %}
46	    </div>
47	  {% endif %}
48	
49	  <!-- Entry bib key -->
50	  <div id="{{ entry.key }}" class="{% if site.enable_publication_thumbnails %}col-sm-8{% else %}col-sm-10{% endif %}">
51	    <!-- Title -->
52	    <div class="title">{{ entry.title }}</div>
53	    <!-- Author -->
54	    <div class="author">
55	      {% assign author_array_size = entry.author_array | size %}
56	
57	      {% assign author_array_limit = author_array_size %}
58	      {% if site.max_author_limit and author_array_size > site.max_author_limit %}
59	        {% assign author_array_limit = site.max_author_limit %}
60	      {% endif %}
61	
62	      {%- for author in entry.author_array limit: author_array_limit -%}
63	        {% assign author_is_self = false %}
64	        {%- assign author_last_name = author.last | regex_replace: '[*∗†‡§¶‖&^]', '' -%}
65	        {%- assign author_last_html = author.last | regex_replace: '([*∗†‡§¶‖&^]+)', '<sup>\1</sup>' -%}
66	        {% if site.scholar.last_name contains author_last_name %}
67	          {% if site.scholar.first_name contains author.first %}
68	            {% assign author_is_self = true %}
69	          {% endif %}
70	        {%- endif -%}
71	        {% assign coauthor_url = null %}
72	        {%- assign clean_last_name = author_last_name | downcase | remove_accents -%}
73	        {% if site.data.coauthors[clean_last_name] %}
74	          {%- for coauthor in site.data.coauthors[clean_last_name] -%}
75	            {% if coauthor.firstname contains author.first %}
76	              {%- assign coauthor_url = coauthor.url -%}
77	              {% break %}
78	            {% endif %}
79	          {% endfor %}
80	        {%- endif -%}
81	
82	        {%- if forloop.length > 1 -%}
83	          {% if forloop.first == false %},&nbsp;{% endif %}
84	          {%- if forloop.last and author_array_limit == author_array_size %}and&nbsp;{% endif -%}
85	        {% endif %}
86	        {%- if author_is_self -%}
87	          <em>
88	            {{- author.first }}
89	            {{ author_last_html -}}
90	          </em>
91	        {%- else -%}
92	          {%- if coauthor_url -%}
93	            <a href="{{ coauthor_url }}">
94	              {{- author.first }}
95	              {{ author_last_html -}}
96	            </a>
97	          {%- else -%}
98	            {{- author.first }}
99	            {{ author_last_html -}}
100	          {% endif %}
101	        {%- endif -%}
102	      {% endfor %}
103	      {%- assign more_authors = author_array_size | minus: author_array_limit -%}
104	
105	      {%- assign more_authors_hide = more_authors | append: ' more author' -%}
106	      {% if more_authors > 0 %}
107	        {%- if more_authors > 1 -%}
108	          {% assign more_authors_hide = more_authors_hide | append: 's' %}
109	        {%- endif -%}
110	        {% assign more_authors_show = '' %}
111	        {%- for author in entry.author_array offset: author_array_limit -%}
112	          {% assign more_authors_show = more_authors_show | append: author.first | append: ' ' | append: author.last %}
113	          {% unless forloop.last %}
114	            {% assign more_authors_show = more_authors_show | append: ', ' %}
115	          {% endunless %}
116	        {%- endfor -%}
117	        {%- assign more_authors_show = more_authors_show | regex_replace: '([*∗†‡§¶‖&^]+)', '<sup>\1</sup>' -%}
118	        , and
119	        <span
120	          class="more-authors"
121	          title="click to view {{ more_authors_hide }}"
122	          onclick="
123	              var element = $(this);
124	              element.attr('title', '');
125	              var more_authors_text = element.text() == '{{ more_authors_hide }}' ? '{{ more_authors_show }}' : '{{ more_authors_hide }}';
126	              var cursorPosition = 0;
127	              var textAdder = setInterval(function(){
128	                element.html(more_authors_text.substring(0, cursorPosition + 1));
129	                if (++cursorPosition == more_authors_text.length){
130	                  clearInterval(textAdder);
131	                }
132	            }, '{{ site.more_authors_animation_delay }}');
133	          "
134	        >
135	          {{- more_authors_hide -}}
136	        </span>
137	      {% endif %}
138	      {% if entry.annotation %}
139	        <i
140	          class="fa-solid fa-circle-info ml-1"
141	          data-toggle="popover"
142	          data-placement="top"
143	          data-html="true"
144	          data-content="{{ entry.annotation | escape }}"
145	        >
146	        </i>
147	      {% endif %}
148	    </div>
149	
150	    <!-- Journal/Book title and date -->
151	    {% assign proceedings = 'inproceedings,incollection' | split: ',' %}
152	    {% assign thesis = 'thesis,mastersthesis,phdthesis' | split: ',' %}
153	    {% if entry.type == 'article' %}
154	      {% capture entrytype %}<em>{{ entry.journal }}</em>{% endcapture %}
155	    {% elsif proceedings contains entry.type %}
156	      {% capture entrytype %}<em>In {{ entry.booktitle }}</em>{% endcapture %}
157	    {% elsif thesis contains entry.type %}
158	      {% capture entrytype %}<em>{{ entry.school }}</em>{% endcapture %}
159	    {% else %}
160	      {% capture entrytype %}{% endcapture %}
161	    {% endif %}
162	    {% if entry.month %}
163	      {% capture entrymonth %}{{ " " }}{{ entry.month | capitalize }}{% endcapture %}
164	    {% endif %}
165	    {% if entry.year %}
166	      {% capture entryyear %}{{ " " }}{{ entry.year }}{% endcapture %}
167	    {% endif %}
168	    {% if entry.location %}
169	      {% capture entrytype %}{{ entrytype }}{{ ", " }}{{ entry.location }}{% endcapture %}
170	    {% endif -%}
171	    {% if entry.additional_info %}
172	      {% capture entrytype %}{{ entrytype }}{{ entry.additional_info | markdownify | remove: '<p>' | remove: '</p>' }}{% endcapture %}
173	    {% endif %}
174	    {% capture hook_exists %}{% file_exists _includes/hook/bib.liquid %}{% endcapture %}
175	    {% if hook_exists == 'true' %}
176	      {% include hook/bib.liquid %}
177	    {% endif %}
178	    {% assign entrytype_text = entrytype | strip_html | strip %}
179	    {% capture periodical %}{{ entrytype }}{% if entrytype_text != "" and entryyear != "" %}, {% endif %}{{ entrymonth }}{{ entryyear }}{% endcapture %}
180	    <div class="periodical">
181	      {{ periodical | strip }}
182	    </div>
183	    <div class="periodical">
184	      {{ entry.note | strip }}
185	    </div>
186	
187	    <!-- Links/Buttons -->
188	    <div class="links">
189	      {% if entry.award %}
190	        <a class="award btn btn-sm z-depth-0" role="button">
191	          {%- if entry.award_name %}{{ entry.award_name }}{% else %}Awarded{% endif -%}
192	        </a>
193	      {% endif %}
194	      {% if entry.abstract %}
195	        <a class="abstract btn btn-sm z-depth-0" role="button">Abs</a>
196	      {% endif %}
197	      {% if entry.doi %}
198	        <a href="https://doi.org/{{ entry.doi }}" class="btn btn-sm z-depth-0" role="button">DOI</a>
199	      {% endif %}
200	      {% if entry.html %}
201	        {% if entry.html contains '://' %}
202	          <a href="{{ entry.html }}" class="btn btn-sm z-depth-0" role="button">Paper</a>
203	        {% else %}
204	          <a href="{{ entry.html | prepend: '/assets/html/' | relative_url }}" class="btn btn-sm z-depth-0" role="button">Paper</a>
205	        {% endif %}
206	      {% elsif entry.arxiv %}
207	        <a href="https://arxiv.org/abs/{{ entry.arxiv }}" class="btn btn-sm z-depth-0" role="button">Paper</a>
208	      {% endif %}
209	      {% if entry.pdf %}
210	        {% if entry.pdf contains '://' %}
211	          <a href="{{ entry.pdf }}" class="btn btn-sm z-depth-0" role="button">PDF</a>
212	        {% else %}
213	          <a href="{{ entry.pdf | prepend: '/assets/pdf/' | relative_url }}" class="btn btn-sm z-depth-0" role="button">PDF</a>
214	        {% endif %}
215	      {% endif %}
216	      {% if entry.supp %}
217	        {% if entry.supp contains '://' %}
218	          <a href="{{ entry.supp }}" class="btn btn-sm z-depth-0" role="button">Supp</a>
219	        {% else %}
220	          <a href="{{ entry.supp | prepend: '/assets/pdf/' | relative_url }}" class="btn btn-sm z-depth-0" role="button">Supp</a>
221	        {% endif %}
222	      {% endif %}
223	      {% if entry.blog %}
224	        <a href="{{ entry.blog }}" class="btn btn-sm z-depth-0" role="button">Blog</a>
225	      {% endif %}
226	      {% if entry.code %}
227	        <a href="{{ entry.code }}" class="btn btn-sm z-depth-0" role="button">Code</a>
228	      {% endif %}
229	      {% if entry.poster %}
230	        {% if entry.poster contains '://' %}
231	          <a href="{{ entry.poster }}" class="btn btn-sm z-depth-0" role="button">Poster</a>
232	        {% else %}
233	          <a href="{{ entry.poster | prepend: '/assets/pdf/' | relative_url }}" class="btn btn-sm z-depth-0" role="button">Poster</a>
234	        {% endif %}
235	      {% endif %}
236	      {% if entry.slides %}
237	        {% if entry.slides contains '://' %}
238	          <a href="{{ entry.slides }}" class="btn btn-sm z-depth-0" role="button">Slides</a>
239	        {% else %}
240	          <a href="{{ entry.slides | prepend: '/assets/pdf/' | relative_url }}" class="btn btn-sm z-depth-0" role="button">Slides</a>
241	        {% endif %}
242	      {% endif %}
243	      {% if entry.website %}
244	        <a href="{{ entry.website }}" class="btn btn-sm z-depth-0" role="button">Website</a>
245	      {% endif %}
246	    </div>
247	    {% if site.enable_publication_badges %}
248	      {% assign entry_has_altmetric_badge = false %}
249	      {% if entry.altmetric and entry.altmetric != 'false' %}
250	        {% assign entry_has_altmetric_badge = true %}
251	      {% endif %}
252	
253	      {% assign entry_has_dimensions_badge = false %}
254	      {% if entry.dimensions and entry.dimensions != 'false' %}
255	        {% assign entry_has_dimensions_badge = true %}
256	      {% endif %}
257	
258	      {% assign entry_has_google_scholar_badge = false %}
259	      {% if entry.google_scholar_id %}
260	        {% assign entry_has_google_scholar_badge = true %}
261	      {% endif %}
262	
263	      {% assign entry_has_github_stars_badge = false %}
264	      {% if entry.github_repo %}
265	        {% assign entry_has_github_stars_badge = true %}
266	      {% endif %}
267	
268	      {% assign entry_has_inspirehep_badge = false %}
269	      {% if entry.inspirehep_id %}
270	        {% assign entry_has_inspirehep_badge = true %}
271	      {% endif %}
272	      {% if entry_has_altmetric_badge or entry_has_dimensions_badge or entry_has_google_scholar_badge or entry_has_github_stars_badge or entry_has_inspirehep_badge %}
273	        <div class="badges">
274	          {% if site.enable_publication_badges.altmetric and entry_has_altmetric_badge %}
275	            <span
276	              class="altmetric-embed"
277	              data-badge-type="2"
278	              data-badge-popover="right"
279	              {% if entry.altmetric != blank and entry.altmetric != 'true' %}
280	                data-altmetric-id="{{ entry.altmetric }}"
281	              {% elsif entry.arxiv %}
282	                data-arxiv-id="{{ entry.arxiv }}"
283	              {% elsif entry.eprint %}
284	                data-arxiv-id="{{ entry.eprint }}"
285	              {% elsif entry.doi %}
286	                data-doi="{{ entry.doi }}"
287	              {% elsif entry.pmid %}
288	                data-pmid="{{ entry.pmid }}"
289	              {% elsif entry.isbn %}
290	                data-isbn="{{ entry.isbn }}"
291	              {% endif %}
292	            ></span>
293	          {% endif %}
294	          {% if site.enable_publication_badges.dimensions and entry_has_dimensions_badge %}
295	            <span
296	              class="__dimensions_badge_embed__"
297	              {% if entry.dimensions != blank and entry.dimensions != 'true' %}
298	                data-id="{{ entry.dimensions }}"
299	              {% elsif entry.doi %}
300	                data-doi="{{ entry.doi }}"
301	              {% else %}
302	                data-pmid="{{ entry.pmid }}"
303	              {% endif %}
304	              data-style="small_rectangle"
305	              data-legend="hover-right"
306	              style="margin-bottom: 3px;"
307	            ></span>
308	          {% endif %}
309	          {% if site.enable_publication_badges.google_scholar and entry_has_google_scholar_badge %}
310	            {% assign gs_count = site.data.scholar_citations.articles[entry.google_scholar_id] | default: 0 %}
311	            <a
312	              href="https://scholar.google.com/citations?view_op=view_citation&hl=en&user={{ site.scholar_userid }}&citation_for_view={{ site.scholar_userid }}:{{ entry.google_scholar_id }}"
313	              aria-label="Google Scholar link"
314	              role="button"
315	              style="display: inline-flex; align-items: stretch; text-decoration: none; border-radius: 4px; overflow: hidden; vertical-align: middle;"
316	            >
317	              <span style="display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.25rem; padding: 0.15rem 0.4rem; background: #f5f0e6; color: #4285F4; font-size: 0.7rem; font-weight: 600; line-height: 1;">
318	                <i class="ai ai-google-scholar" style="font-size: 0.8rem;"></i> Scholar
319	              </span>
320	              <span style="display: inline-flex; align-items: center; padding: 0.15rem 0.4rem; background: #4285F4; color: #fff; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 700; line-height: 1;">
321	                {{ gs_count }}
322	              </span>
323	            </a>
324	          {% endif %}
325	          {% if site.enable_publication_badges.github_stars and entry_has_github_stars_badge %}
326	            {% assign star_count = site.data.github_stars.repos[entry.github_repo] | default: 0 %}
327	            <a
328	              href="https://github.com/{{ entry.github_repo }}"
329	              aria-label="GitHub repository"
330	              role="button"
331	              style="display: inline-flex; align-items: stretch; text-decoration: none; border-radius: 4px; overflow: hidden; vertical-align: middle;"
332	            >
333	              <span style="display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.25rem; padding: 0.15rem 0.4rem; background: #f5f0e6; color: #181717; font-size: 0.7rem; font-weight: 600; line-height: 1;">
334	                <i class="fa-brands fa-github" style="font-size: 0.8rem; color: #181717;"></i> Github
335	              </span>
336	              <span style="display: inline-flex; align-items: center; padding: 0.15rem 0.4rem; background: #181717; color: #fff; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 700; line-height: 1;">
337	                {{ star_count }} ★
338	              </span>
339	            </a>
340	          {% endif %}
341	          {% if site.enable_publication_badges.inspirehep and entry_has_inspirehep_badge %}
342	            <a
343	              href="https://inspirehep.net/literature/{{ entry.inspirehep_id }}"
344	              aria-label="Inspirehep link"
345	              role="button"
346	            >
347	              <img
348	                src="https://img.shields.io/badge/inspire-{% inspirehep_citations entry.inspirehep_id %}-001628?logo=inspire&logoColor=001628&labelColor=beige"
349	                alt="{% inspirehep_citations entry.inspirehep_id %} InspireHEP citations"
350	              >
351	            </a>
352	          {% endif %}
353	        </div>
354	      {% endif %}
355	    {% endif %}
356	
357	    {% if entry.award %}
358	      <!-- Hidden Award block -->
359	      <div class="award hidden d-print-inline">
360	        <p>{{ entry.award | markdownify }}</p>
361	      </div>
362	    {% endif %}
363	
364	    {% if entry.abstract %}
365	      <!-- Hidden abstract block -->
366	      <div class="abstract hidden">
367	        <p>{{ entry.abstract }}</p>
368	      </div>
369	    {% endif %}
370	
371	    {% if entry.bibtex_show %}
372	      <!-- Hidden bibtex block -->
373	      <div class="bibtex hidden">
374	        {% highlight bibtex %}
375	        {{- entry.bibtex | hideCustomBibtex -}}
376	        {% endhighlight %}
377	      </div>
378	    {% endif %}
379	
380	    {% if entry.video and site.enable_video_embedding %}
381	      <!-- Hidden video block -->
382	      <div class="abstract hidden">
383	        <div style="text-align: center;">{% include video.liquid path=entry.video class="img-fluid rounded z-depth-1" controls=true %}</div>
384	      </div>
385	    {% endif %}
386	  </div>
387	</div>
388
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
30	repo_theme_light: default # https://github.com/anuraghazra/github-readme-stats/blob/master/themes/README.md
31	repo_theme_dark: dark # https://github.com/anuraghazra/github-readme-stats/blob/master/themes/README.md
32	repo_trophies:
33	  enabled: true
34	  theme_light: flat # https://github.com/ryo-ma/github-profile-trophy
35	  theme_dark: gitdimmed # https://github.com/ryo-ma/github-profile-trophy
36	
37	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
38	# RSS Feed
39	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
40	# will use title and url fields
41	# Take a look to https://github.com/jekyll/jekyll-feed for more customization
42	
43	rss_icon: false
44	
45	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
46	# Layout
47	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
48	
49	navbar_fixed: true
50	footer_fixed: true
51	search_enabled: true
52	socials_in_search: true
53	bib_search: true
54	
55	# Dimensions
56	max_width: 930px
57	
58	# TODO: add layout settings (single page vs. multi-page)
59	
60	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
61	# Open Graph & Schema.org
62	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
63	# Display links to the page with a preview object on social media.
64	# see https://schema.org/docs/faq.html for more information
65	serve_og_meta: false # Include Open Graph meta tags in the HTML head
66	serve_schema_org: false # Include Schema.org in the HTML head
67	og_image: # The site-wide (default for all links) Open Graph preview image
68	
69	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
70	# Social integration
71	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
72	
73	acm_id: # your dl.acm.org/profile/id
74	blogger_url: # your blogger URL
75	bluesky_url: # your bluesky URL
76	dblp_url: # your DBLP profile url
77	discord_id: # your discord id (18-digit unique numerical identifier)
78	facebook_id: # your facebook id
79	flickr_id: # your flickr id
80	github_username: BillWan-zzzyyy # your GitHub user name
81	gitlab_username: # your GitLab user name
82	ieee_id: # your ieeexplore.ieee.org/author/id
83	inspirehep_id: # Inspire HEP author ID
84	instagram_id: # your instagram id
85	kaggle_id: # your kaggle id
86	keybase_username: # your keybase user name
87	lastfm_id: # your lastfm id
88	lattes_id: # your ID on Lattes (Brazilian Lattes CV)
89	linkedin_username: zhengyang-wan-5818bb1bb # your LinkedIn user name
90	mastodon_username: # your mastodon instance+username in the format instance.tld/@username
91	medium_username: # your Medium username
92	orcid_id: 0009-0003-2691-8389 # your ORCID ID
93	osf_id: # your OSF ID
94	pinterest_id: # your pinterest id
95	publons_id: # your ID on Publons
96	quora_username: # your Quora username
97	research_gate_profile: # your profile on ResearchGate
98	scholar_userid: 6m8LnLUAAAAJ # your Google Scholar ID
99	scopus_id: # your profile on Scopus
100	semanticscholar_id: # your Semantic Scholar ID
101	spotify_id: # your spotify id
102	stackoverflow_id: # your stackoverflow id
103	telegram_username: # your Telegram user name
104	unsplash_id: # your unsplash id
105	wechat_qr: wechat-qr.png # filename of your wechat qr-code saved as an image (e.g., wechat-qr.png if saved to assets/img/wechat-qr.png)
106	whatsapp_number: # your WhatsApp number (full phone number in international format. Omit any zeroes, brackets, or dashes when adding the phone number in international format.)
107	wikidata_id: # your wikidata id
108	wikipedia_id: # your wikipedia id (Case sensitive)
109	work_url: # work page URL
110	x_username: # your X handle
111	youtube_id: # your youtube channel id (youtube.com/@<youtube_id>)
112	zotero_username: # your zotero username
113	
114	contact_note: >
115	
116	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
117	# Analytics and search engine verification
118	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
119	
120	# For Google Analytics, see https://support.google.com/analytics/answer/10447272?hl=en&ref_topic=14088998&sjid=5129943941510317771-SA#zippy=%2Cgoogle-sites
121	# and follow the instructions for Google Sites. You will need to create a Google Analytics property and copy the Google tag ID.
122	google_analytics: # your Google Analytics measurement ID (format: G-XXXXXXXXXX)
123	cronitor_analytics: # cronitor RUM analytics site ID (format: XXXXXXXXX)
124	pirsch_analytics: # your Pirsch analytics site ID (length 32 characters)
125	openpanel_analytics: # your Openpanel analytics client ID (format: XXXXXXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXXXXXXXXXX)
126	
127	# For Google Search Console, see https://support.google.com/webmasters/answer/9008080?hl=en#meta_tag_verification&zippy=%2Chtml-tag
128	google_site_verification: # your google-site-verification ID (Google Search Console)
129	bing_site_verification: # out your bing-site-verification ID (Bing Webmaster)
130	
131	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
132	# Blog
133	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
134	
135	blog_name: al-folio # blog_name will be displayed in your blog page
136	blog_description: a simple whitespace theme for academics
137	permalink: /blog/:year/:title/
138	lsi: false # produce an index for related posts
139	
140	# Pagination
141	pagination:
142	  enabled: true
143	
144	related_blog_posts:
145	  enabled: true
146	  max_related: 10
147	
148	# Giscus comments (RECOMMENDED)
149	# Follow instructions on https://giscus.app/ to setup for your repo to fill out the information below.
150	giscus:
151	  repo: # <your-github-user-name>/<your-github-repo-name>
152	  repo_id: # leave empty or specify your repo_id (see https://giscus.app/)
153	  category: Comments # name of the category under which discussions will be created
154	  category_id: # leave empty or specify your category_id (see https://giscus.app/)
155	  mapping: title # identify discussions by post title
156	  strict: 1 # use strict identification mode
157	  reactions_enabled: 1 # enable (1) or disable (0) emoji reactions
158	  input_position: bottom # whether to display input form below (bottom) or above (top) the comments
159	  theme: preferred_color_scheme # name of the color scheme (preferred works well with al-folio light/dark mode)
160	  emit_metadata: 0
161	  lang: en
162	
163	# Disqus comments (DEPRECATED)
164	disqus_shortname: al-folio # put your disqus shortname
165	# https://help.disqus.com/en/articles/1717111-what-s-a-shortname
166	
167	# External sources.
168	# If you have blog posts published on medium.com or other external sources,
169	# you can display them in your blog by adding a link to the RSS feed.
170	#external_sources:
171	#  - name: medium.com
172	 #   rss_url: https://medium.com/@al-folio/feed
173	#  - name: Google Blog
174	  #  posts:
175	   #   - url: https://blog.google/technology/ai/google-gemini-update-flash-ai-assistant-io-2024/
176	    #    published_date: 2024-05-14
177	
178	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
179	# Newsletter
180	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
181	
182	newsletter:
183	  enabled: false
184	  endpoint: # your loops endpoint (e.g., https://app.loops.so/api/newsletter-form/YOUR-ENDPOINT)
185	  # https://loops.so/docs/forms/custom-form
186	
187	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
188	# Collections
189	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
190	
191	collections:
192	  news:
193	    defaults:
194	      layout: post
195	    output: true
196	    permalink: /:collection/:title/
197	  projects:
198	    output: true
199	    permalink: /:collection/:title/
200	
201	announcements:
202	  enabled: true
203	  scrollable: true # adds a vertical scroll bar if there are more than 3 news items
204	  limit: 7 # leave blank to include all the news in the `_news` folder
205	
206	latest_posts:
207	  enabled: true
208	  scrollable: true # adds a vertical scroll bar if there are more than 3 new posts items
209	  limit: 3 # leave blank to include all the blog posts
210	
211	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
212	# Jekyll settings
213	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
214	
215	# Markdown and syntax highlight
216	markdown: kramdown
217	highlighter: rouge
218	kramdown:
219	  input: GFM
220	  syntax_highlighter_opts:
221	    css_class: "highlight"
222	    span:
223	      line_numbers: false
224	    block:
225	      line_numbers: false
226	      start_line: 1
227	
228	# Includes & excludes
229	include: ["_pages"]
230	exclude:
231	  - bin/
232	  - CONTRIBUTING.md
233	  - CUSTOMIZE.md
234	  - Dockerfile
235	  - docker-compose.yml
236	  - docker-compose-slim.yml
237	  - FAQ.md
238	  - Gemfile
239	  - Gemfile.lock
240	  - INSTALL.md
241	  - LICENSE
242	  - lighthouse_results/
243	  - package.json
244	  - package-lock.json
245	  - _pages/about_einstein.md
246	  - purgecss.config.js
247	  - README.md
248	  - readme_preview/
249	  - vendor
250	keep_files:
251	  - CNAME
252	  - .nojekyll
253	
254	# Plug-ins
255	plugins:
256	  - jekyll-archives
257	  - jekyll-email-protect
258	  - jekyll-feed
259	  - jekyll-get-json
260	  - jekyll-imagemagick
261	  - jekyll-jupyter-notebook
262	  - jekyll-link-attributes
263	  - jekyll-minifier
264	  - jekyll-paginate-v2
265	  - jekyll-regex-replace
266	  - jekyll/scholar
267	  - jekyll-sitemap
268	  - jekyll-tabs
269	  - jekyll-toc
270	  - jekyll-twitter-plugin
271	  - jemoji
272	
273	# Sitemap settings
274	defaults:
275	  - scope:
276	      path: "assets"
277	    values:
278	      sitemap: false
279	
280	sass:
281	  style: compressed
282	
283	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
284	# Jekyll Minifier
285	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
286	
287	jekyll-minifier:
288	  exclude: ["robots.txt", "assets/js/search/*.js", "assets/libs/**/*"]
289	  uglifier_args:
290	    harmony: true
291	
292	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
293	# Jekyll Archives
294	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
295	
296	jekyll-archives:
297	  enabled: [year, tags, categories] # enables year, tag and category archives (remove if you need to disable one of them).
298	  layouts:
299	    year: archive-year
300	    tag: archive-tag
301	    category: archive-category
302	  permalinks:
303	    year: "/blog/:year/"
304	    tag: "/blog/tag/:name/"
305	    category: "/blog/category/:name/"
306	
307	display_tags: ["formatting", "images", "links", "math", "code", "blockquotes"] # these tags will be displayed on the front page of your blog
308	display_categories: ["external-services"] # these categories will be displayed on the front page of your blog
309	
310	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
311	# Jekyll Scholar
312	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
313	
314	scholar:
315	  last_name: [Wan]
316	  first_name: [Zhengyang]
317	
318	  style: apa
319	  locale: en
320	
321	  source: /_bibliography/
322	  bibliography: papers.bib
323	  bibliography_template: bib
324	  # Note: if you have latex math in your bibtex, the latex filter
325	  # preprocessing may conflict with MathJAX if the latter is enabled.
326	  # See https://github.com/alshedivat/al-folio/issues/357.
327	  bibtex_filters: [latex, smallcaps, superscript]
328	
329	  replace_strings: true
330	  join_strings: true
331	
332	  details_dir: bibliography
333	  details_link: Details
334	
335	  query: "@*"
336	  group_by: year
337	  group_order: descending
338	
339	# Display different badges withs stats for your publications
340	# Customize badge behavior in _layouts/bib.liquid
341	enable_publication_badges:
342	  altmetric: true # Altmetric badge (Customization options: https://badge-docs.altmetric.com/index.html)
343	  dimensions: true # Dimensions badge (Customization options: https://badge.dimensions.ai/)
344	  google_scholar: true # Google Scholar badge (https://scholar.google.com/intl/en/scholar/citations.html)
345	  github_stars: true # GitHub stars badge for papers with associated repos
346	  inspirehep: true # Inspire HEP badge (https://help.inspirehep.net/knowledge-base/citation-metrics/)
347	
348	# Filter out certain bibtex entry keywords used internally from the bib output
349	filtered_bibtex_keywords:
350	  [
351	    abbr,
352	    abstract,
353	    additional_info,
354	    altmetric,
355	    annotation,
356	    arxiv,
357	    award,
358	    award_name,
359	    bibtex_show,
360	    blog,
361	    code,
362	    github_repo,
363	    google_scholar_id,
364	    html,
365	    inspirehep_id,
366	    pdf,
367	    poster,
368	    preview,
369	    selected,
370	    slides,
371	    supp,
372	    video,
373	    website,
374	  ]
375	
376	# Maximum number of authors to be shown for each publication (more authors are visible on click)
377	max_author_limit: 3 # leave blank to always show all authors
378	more_authors_animation_delay: 10 # more authors are revealed on click using animation; smaller delay means faster animation
379	
380	# Enables publication thumbnails. If disabled, none of the publications will display thumbnails, even if specified in the bib entry.
381	enable_publication_thumbnails: true
382	
383	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
384	# Jekyll Link Attributes
385	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
386	
387	# These are the defaults
388	external_links:
389	  enabled: true
390	  rel: external nofollow noopener
391	  target: _blank
392	  exclude:
393	
394	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
395	# Responsive WebP Images
396	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
397	
398	# MAKE SURE imagemagick is installed and on your PATH before enabling imagemagick. In a terminal, run:
399	#   convert -version
400	imagemagick:
401	  enabled: true # enables responsive images for your site (recommended, see https://github.com/alshedivat/al-folio/issues/537)
402	  widths:
403	    - 480
404	    - 800
405	    - 1400
406	  input_directories:
407	    - assets/img/
408	  input_formats:
409	    - ".jpg"
410	    - ".jpeg"
411	    - ".png"
412	    - ".tiff"
413	    - ".gif"
414	  output_formats:
415	    webp: "-quality 85"
416	
417	# Lazy loading images
418	#   If you enable lazy loading, all images will add the loading="lazy" attribute.
419	#   This will make your site load faster, but it may not be supported in all browsers.
420	#   You can also set loading="" to other values for specific images to override the default behavior.
421	#   Options: "auto", "eager", "lazy"
422	#   See https://web.dev/browser-level-image-lazy-loading/ for more information.
423	lazy_loading_images: true # enables lazy loading of images (recommended)
424	
425	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
426	# Optional Features
427	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
428	
429	enable_google_analytics: false # enables google analytics
430	enable_cronitor_analytics: false # enables cronitor RUM analytics
431	enable_pirsch_analytics: false # enables Pirsch analytics (https://pirsch.io/)
432	enable_openpanel_analytics: false # enables Openpanel analytics (https://openpanel.dev/)
433	enable_google_verification: false # enables google site verification
434	enable_bing_verification: false # enables bing site verification
435	enable_masonry: true # enables automatic project cards arrangement
436	enable_math: true # enables math typesetting (uses MathJax)
437	enable_tooltips: false # enables automatic tooltip links generated for each section titles on pages and posts
438	enable_darkmode: true # enables switching between light/dark modes
439	enable_navbar_social: false # enables displaying social links in the navbar on the about page
440	enable_project_categories: true # enables categorization of projects into multiple categories
441	enable_medium_zoom: true # enables image zoom feature (as on medium.com)
442	enable_progressbar: true # enables a horizontal progress bar linked to the vertical scroll position
443	enable_video_embedding: false # enables video embedding for bibtex entries. If false, the button opens the video link in a new window.
444
```

```text
@article{huang2026sim2real,
  title={Sim2Real-AD: A Modular Sim-to-Real Framework for Deploying VLM-Guided Reinforcement Learning in Real-World Autonomous Driving},
  author={Huang, Zilin and Wan, Zhengyang and Sheng, Zihao and Wang, Boyue and You, Junwei and Leng, Yue and Chen✉, Sikai},
  journal={arXiv preprint arXiv:2604.03497},
  year={2026},
  bibtex_show={true},
  abbr={arXiv},
  arxiv={2604.03497},
  priority={0},
  google_scholar_id={W7OEmFMy1HYC},
  preview={sim2realad.webp},
  abstract={Deploying reinforcement learning policies trained in simulation to real autonomous vehicles remains a fundamental challenge, particularly for VLM-guided RL frameworks whose policies are typically learned with simulatornative observations and simulator-coupled action semantics that are unavailable on physical platforms. This paper
presents Sim2Real-AD, a modular framework for zero-shot sim-to-real transfer of CARLA-trained VLM-guided RL
policies to full-scale vehicles without any real-world RL training data.},
  annotation={* Equal Contribution<br>✉ Corresponding Author},
  selected={true}
}


@article{huang2026drivevlmrl,
  title={DriveVLM-RL: Neuroscience-Inspired Reinforcement Learning with Vision-Language Models for Safe and Deployable Autonomous Driving},
  author={Huang, Zilin and Sheng, Zihao and Wan, Zhengyang and Qu, Yansong and You, Junwei and Jiang, Sicong and Chen✉, Sikai},
  journal={arXiv preprint arXiv:2603.18315},
  year={2026},
  bibtex_show={true},
  abbr={arXiv},
  arxiv={2603.18315},
  priority={0},
  google_scholar_id={Y0pCki6q_DkC},
  preview={drivevlmrl.webp},
  abstract={Ensuring safe decision-making in autonomous vehicles remains a fundamental challenge despite rapid advances in end-to-end learning approaches. Traditional reinforcement learning (RL) methods rely on manually engineered rewards or sparse collision signals, which fail to capture the rich contextual understanding required for safe driving and make unsafe exploration unavoidable in real-world settings. Recent vision-language models (VLMs) offer promising semantic understanding capabilities; however, their high inference latency and susceptibility to hallucination hinder direct application to real-time vehicle control. To address these limitations, this paper proposes DriveVLM-RL, a neuroscience-inspired framework that integrates VLMs into RL through a dual-pathway architecture for safe and deployable autonomous driving. The framework decomposes semantic reward learning into a Static Pathway for continuous spatial safety assessment using CLIP-based contrasting language goals, and a Dynamic Pathway for attention-gated multi-frame semantic risk reasoning using a lightweight detector and a large VLM. A hierarchical reward synthesis mechanism fuses semantic signals with vehicle states, while an asynchronous training pipeline decouples expensive VLM inference from environment interaction. All VLM components are used only during offline training and are removed at deployment, ensuring real-time feasibility. Experiments in the CARLA simulator show significant improvements in collision avoidance, task success, and generalization across diverse traffic scenarios, including strong robustness under settings without explicit collision penalties. These results demonstrate that DriveVLM-RL provides a practical paradigm for integrating foundation models into autonomous driving without compromising real-time feasibility.},
  annotation={* Equal Contribution<br>✉ Corresponding Author},
  selected={true}
}


@article{huang2025sky,
  title={Sky-Drive: A Distributed Multi-Agent Simulation Platform for Socially-Aware and Human-AI Collaborative Future Transportation},
  author={Huang*, Zilin and Sheng*, Zihao and Wan*, Zhengyang and Qu, Yansong and Luo, Yuhao and Wang, Boyue and Li, Pei and Chen, Yen-Jung and Chen, Jiancong and Long, Keke and Meng, Jiayi and Leng, Yue and Chen✉, Sikai},
  journal={Journal of Intelligent and Connected Vehicles},
  year={2025},
  bibtex_show={true},  
  abbr={JICV},
  priority={1},
  year={2025},
  abstract={Recent advances in autonomous system simulation platforms have significantly enhanced the safe and scalable testing of driving policies. However, existing simulators do not yet fully meet the needs of future transportation research, particularly in modeling socially-aware driving agents and enabling effective human-AI collaboration. This paper introduces Sky-Drive, a novel distributed multi-agent simulation platform that addresses these limitations through four key innovations: (a) a distributed architecture for synchronized simulation across multiple terminals; (b) a multi-modal human-in-the-loop framework integrating diverse sensors to collect rich behavioral data; (c) a human-AI collaboration mechanism supporting continuous and adaptive knowledge exchange; and (d) a digital twin (DT) framework for constructing high-fidelity virtual replicas of real-world transportation environments. Sky-Drive supports diverse applications such as autonomous vehicle (AV)-vulnerable road user (VRU) interaction modeling, human-in-the-loop training, socially-aware reinforcement learning, personalized driving policy, and customized scenario generation. Future extensions will incorporate foundation models for context-aware decision support and hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) testing for real-world validation. By bridging scenario generation, data collection, algorithm training, and hardware integration, Sky-Drive has the potential to become a foundational platform for the next generation of socially-aware and human-centered autonomous transportation researc},
  html={https://sky-lab-uw.github.io/Sky-Drive-website/},
```

```text
1	/*******************************************************************************
2	 * Styles for the base elements of the theme.
3	 ******************************************************************************/
4	
5	// Typography
6	
7	body {
8	  font-family: 'Source Serif 4', ui-serif, Georgia, 'Times New Roman', serif;
9	}
10	
11	h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6 {
12	  font-family: 'Inter', -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif;
13	}
14	
15	code, pre, kbd, samp, tt {
16	  font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Monaco, Consolas, 'Liberation Mono', monospace;
17	}
18	
19	p,
20	h1,
21	h2,
22	h3,
23	h4,
24	h5,
25	h6,
26	em,
27	div,
28	li,
29	span,
30	strong {
31	  color: var(--global-text-color);
32	}
33	
34	h2 {
35	  font-weight: 600;
36	}
37	
38	h6 {
39	  font-size: 1.5rem;
40	  font-weight: 600;
41	  display: inline-block; /* 让 h2 的宽度贴合内容，下划线长度会以此为基准 */
42	  position: relative;    /* 关键：为伪元素的绝对定位提供一个锚点 */
43	  padding-bottom: 8px;   /* 可选：为下划线留出足够的空间，避免重叠 */
44	}
45	
46	/* 创建并定义下划线本体 */
47	h6::after {
48	  content: '';         /* 伪元素必须有 content 属性 */
49	  position: absolute;
50	  bottom: 0;           /* 定位在 h2 元素的底部 */
51	  left: 0;             /* 从 h2 元素的左侧开始 */
52	
53	  /* --- 自定义部分 --- */
54	  width: 60px;             /* 1. 自定义长度：可以是百分比(如 50%)或固定值(如 80px) */
55	  height: 6px;             /* 2. 自定义粗细/宽度 */
56	  background-color: var(--global-theme-color);/* 3. 自定义颜色 */
57	}
58	
59	hr {
60	  border-top: 1px solid var(--global-divider-color);
61	}
62	
63	table {
64	  td,
65	  th {
66	    font-size: 1rem;
67	    padding: 1px 1rem 1px 0;
68	  }
69	
70	  th {
71	    font-weight: bold;
72	  }
73	}
74	
75	a,
76	table.table a {
77	  color: var(--global-theme-color);
78	
79	  &:hover {
80	    color: var(--global-theme-color);
81	    text-decoration: underline;
82	  }
83	
84	  &:hover:after :not(.nav-item.dropdown) {
85	    width: 100%;
86	  }
87	}
88	
89	.table-dark {
90	  background-color: transparent;
91	  &.table-bordered {
92	    border: 1px solid var(--global-divider-color) !important;
93	  }
94	}
95	
96	blockquote {
97	  background: var(--global-quoto-block-bg);
```

---

## HOMEPAGE & LAYOUT STRUCTURE REPORT

### **Current Homepage (about.md) — Visible Sections & Order**

The homepage (`_pages/about.md` → `_layouts/about.liquid` → `_layouts/default.liquid`) displays sections in this sequence:

1. **Title bar** — "ZHENGYANG WAN" in bold + subtitle "Ph.D. student at The University of Wisconsin-Madison CEE" with red highlight (line 5-6)
2. **Social icons** — GitHub, Google Scholar, ORCID, LinkedIn, etc. (about.liquid:18-21, social.liquid:1-133)
3. **Profile card** — Right-aligned, non-circular photo (DSCF1344.webp, about.md:8-13, about.liquid:30-50)
4. **Bio text** — Dense multi-paragraph prose (~250 words) with inline institution logos (about.md:19-31)
5. **Horizontal divider** (about.liquid:56)
6. **Recent News** — Table with date + inline/linked items, max 7 entries (about.liquid:59-64, news.liquid:1-34)
7. **Horizontal divider** (about.liquid:80)
8. **Selected Publications** — BibTeX filtered by `selected={true}` (3 papers shown, about.liquid:83-88, selected_papers.liquid:1-3)
9. **Visitor count heatmap** — ClusterMaps widget (about.liquid:96-100)

No hero image, no featured projects strip, no timeline, no testimonials.

---

### **Navigation Structure**

**Header/Navbar** (`_includes/header.liquid:1-150`, fixed-top per `_config.yml:49`):
- Logo links to home only on non-home pages (header.liquid:5-22)
- Right-aligned navbar items:
  - **About** (home, always visible)
  - **Publications** (nav_order: 2, _pages/publications.md:6-7)
  - **Projects** (nav_order: 3, _pages/projects.md:5-6)
  - Other pages: CV, News, Blog, Repositories, Teaching, Profiles (inferred from _pages glob)
  - **Dropdown support** (header.liquid:63-99) — unused currently
  - **Search toggle** (Ctrl+K, header.liquid:122-126, enabled by _config.yml:51)
  - **Dark/Light theme toggle** (header.liquid:130-136, enabled by _config.yml:438)
  - **Progress bar** (header.liquid:144-148, enabled by _config.yml:442)

No navbar social icons (enable_navbar_social: false, _config.yml:439).

---

### **Footer** (`_includes/footer.liquid:1-35`)

Fixed bottom (sticky-bottom-footer class). Contains:
- Copyright year + name
- Attribution: "Powered by Jekyll with al-folio theme. Hosted by GitHub Pages."
- Last updated timestamp (if enabled, _config.yml:21)
- No social links, no newsletter signup

---

### **Includes & Data Sources**

| File | Purpose | Notes |
|------|---------|-------|
| `_includes/social.liquid` | Renders icon links to ~40 platforms (GitHub, Google Scholar, ORCID, LinkedIn, WeChat, etc.) | Conditional on `_config.yml` values; email masked |
| `_includes/news.liquid` | Renders `_news/*.md` entries as a scrollable table (max-height: 60vw if scrollable enabled) | Limit: 7 items; date + title/inline content |
| `_includes/selected_papers.liquid` | Renders BibTeX entries with `selected={true}` via jekyll-scholar plugin | Line 2: `{% bibliography --query @*[selected=true]* %}` |
| `_includes/figure.liquid` | Lazy-loads profile image with WebP responsive sizing | Cache-bust, z-depth-1 shadow, rounded corners |

---

### **Config Highlights** (`_config.yml`)

**Identity:**
- Name: Zhengyang Wan (first_name, last_name; title: blank → full name used)
- Email: zhengyang.wan@wisc.edu
- Description: "Personal academic website..."
- Keywords: "autonomous driving, intelligent transportation, vision-language model"

**Features Enabled/Disabled:**
- `announcements.enabled: true` — Shows news section with 7-item limit, scrollable if >5
- `latest_posts.enabled: true` (commented out in about.liquid:66-76)
- `enable_darkmode: true` — Light/dark toggle in navbar
- `enable_search: true` — Ctrl+K search modal
- `navbar_fixed: true` — Sticky header
- `footer_fixed: true` — Fixed bottom footer
- `enable_publication_badges: true` — Altmetric, Dimensions, Google Scholar badges on papers
- `enable_publication_thumbnails: true` — Paper preview images
- `enable_math: true` — MathJax enabled
- `enable_medium_zoom: true` — Click-to-zoom images
- `enable_progressbar: true` — Scroll position bar
- `repo_trophies.enabled: true` — GitHub profile trophies (unused on homepage)

**Max content width:** 930px (`max_width: 930px`)

**Bibliography:** APA style, grouped by year (descending), with custom badges/links (lines 314-346).

---

### **News & Bibliography Content Density**

**News (_news/*.md):** 11 items spanning 2018–2025. Example:
- announcement_1.md: "I began pursuing my Bachelor's degree at Tongji University.🧐" (inline, no link)
- announcement_11 and others: Likely mix of inline text + linked items

**Bibliography (papers.bib top 60 lines):** 
- 3 papers shown as "selected"
- Titles: Sim2Real-AD, DriveVLM-RL, Sky-Drive (all 2025–2026, arXiv/JICV)
- Rich metadata: arxiv ID, preview image, abstract, GitHub repo, video link, annotation
- Publication badges enabled (Altmetric, Dimensions, Google Scholar)

**Profile image:** DSCF1344.webp (sunset beach photo, casual, professional), right-aligned, responsive sizing with Sass variables.

---

### **Non-Standard Customizations vs Stock al-folio**

1. **Inline logos in bio** — Custom inline `<img>` tags for UW-Madison, Sky-Lab, Tongji, Bucky icons (about.md:19-31)
2. **Red highlight text** — `.highlight-text` class; custom color #c5051c (about.md:5, _layout.scss:41-44)
3. **Visitor heatmap** — ClusterMaps widget added at bottom of about.liquid (lines 96-100)
4. **Custom profile location** — "Chicago, US, 2025" in more_info block (about.md:12-13)
5. **Newsletter disabled** — (`newsletter.enabled: false`, _config.yml:183)
6. **Blog posts section commented out** — Latest_posts include removed from about.liquid (lines 66-76)

**No custom plugins** (uses standard al-folio set: jekyll-scholar, jekyll-feed, jekyll-archives, jemoji, etc.).

---

### **Visual Weaknesses & Missing Patterns**

1. **No hero section** — Homepage starts with plain title; no eye-catching banner, background image, or tagline callout
2. **Dense text-wall bio** — 31 lines of paragraph text before any visual breaks; very academic/verbose
3. **Minimal visual hierarchy** — Profile card is only visual relief; rest is headings + text tables
4. **Missing CTAs** — No "Recent Work" button, "Download CV" link, or "Contact Me" button above fold
5. **No featured projects strip** — Only three publications shown; projects page is separate
6. **Inactive latest_posts section** — Blog enabled but hidden on homepage
7. **No timeline** — Education/career milestones listed inline rather than as interactive timeline
8. **Static visitor counter** — Heatmap at bottom is ornamental, not integrated
9. **No testimonials or achievements** — No quote section, no badge/medal summary (despite having GitHub trophies enabled)
10. **Missing "News" context** — News items are bare dates + titles; no category/tag filters
11. **No modern patterns** — No gradient overlays, no card shadows on main sections, minimal use of color (only red subtitle + default blue links)

---

| `_pages/about.md` | 1–31 (front matter + bio content) |

---

**Summary:** Your site is a functional, feature-rich academic portfolio using al-folio defaults with light customization (inline logos, red subtitle, visitor widget). It's **information-dense but visually flat**—ideal for a research-focused audience but lacking modern visual appeal (hero, gradients, clear hierarchy, featured CTAs). A redesign should balance academic credibility with contemporary UX patterns (hero with tagline, visual card sections, timeline, project highlights, contact CTA).
