Zhengyang Wan is a first-year Ph.D. student in the Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering at UW–Madison, joined Sky-Lab in Fall 2025 under Dr. Sikai (Sky) Chen.

Prior: M.S. Transportation Engineering at Tongji (2025), B.E. Vehicle Engineering at Tongji Institute of Rail Transit (2022). Advisors at Tongji: Prof. Hechao Zhou, Prof. Jimin Zhang.

Research focus: **autonomous driving**, **vision-language-action models**, **intelligent transportation systems**. Major project: [SkyDrive](https://github.com/BillWan-zzzyyy/Sky-Drive), an open-source multi-agent AD/ITS platform.

Communicates primarily in Chinese. Maintains the GitHub Pages personal site at `BillWan-zzzyyy.github.io` using the al-folio Jekyll theme.

```

---
name: Site design direction — editorial base + tech accents, UW red brand
description: Locked visual direction for this al-folio site: Academic Editorial (Nature/Stripe/Distill) as base with Modern Tech (Karpathy-style mono metadata, hairline dark mode) accents. Keep UW red #c5050c as single brand accent in both light + dark modes.
type: feedback
---

For this site (`BillWan-zzzyyy.github.io`), the user locked the following design direction during the 2026-04-18 redesign session:

- **Aesthetic:** Academic Editorial base (serif display/body, generous whitespace, hairline rules, no boxed cards) + Modern Tech accents (mono metadata, mono ⌘K pill, cool-ink dark mode, subtle transitions).
- **Hero layout:** keep the existing title-left + avatar-right structure. Do not propose full-bleed hero images or split-hero gradients.
- **Color system:** UW red `#c5050c` stays the single brand accent in both light and dark modes (override al-folio's default cyan-in-dark behavior). Neutrals use Tailwind-style 9-step scales; dark mode uses cool-ink (`#0f1216` → `#e8eaed`), never pure black.
- **Typography:** Source Serif 4 for display + body, Inter for UI/navbar/labels, JetBrains Mono for metadata (year, venue, ⭐ counts, eyebrow, date).
- **Scope of future changes:** templates (`_layouts/*.liquid`, `_includes/*.liquid`) + Sass (`_sass/*.scss`) are fair game; Jekyll pipeline / plugins / Bootstrap foundation stay untouched.
- **Out of scope pages (not shown on site):** Repositories, Teaching. Do not redesign these unless the user explicitly re-enables them.

**Why:** User explicitly chose this over Modern Tech (too casual for PhD credibility), Research-lab (too heavy for a single student), and polish-only (underdelivers on "full overhaul" request). Rationale: academic readability is the page's job; tech accents signal the AI/AD research focus without compromising credibility.

**How to apply:** When editing styles, keep red use restricted to links, navbar active state, `author-self` in publications, `.selected-dot`, and CTA buttons. Avoid introducing secondary accent colors. When adding components, prefer hairline borders + mono metadata over boxed cards.

```

19	Hello there!🤺
20	
21	I am a first-year **Ph.D. student** in the Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering at the <img src="../assets/img/uwmadison.png" class="inline-logo" alt="UW–Madison"/>[University of Wisconsin-Madison](https://www.wisc.edu/) (UW-Madison). I joined <img src="../assets/img/skylab.png" class="inline-logo" alt="Sky-Lab"/>[Sky-Lab](https://sky-lab-uw.github.io/) at UW-Madison in Fall 2025 under the guidance of Dr.[Sikai (Sky) Chen](https://sky-lab-uw.github.io/people/). On Wisconsin!<img src="../assets/img/bucky.png" class="inline-logo" alt="Bucky"/>
22	
23	Before joining UW-Madison, I obtained my M.S. in Transportation Engineering at [College of Transportation](https://tjjt.tongji.edu.cn/), <img src="../assets/img/tongji.png" class="inline-logo" alt="Tongji"/>Tongji University in 2025, and my B.E. in Vehicle Engineering at [Institute of Rail Transit](https://railway.tongji.edu.cn/main.htm), Tongji University in 2022. I am very fortunate and honored to be advised by Prof. [Hechao Zhou](https://tjjt.tongji.edu.cn/info/2943/10933.htm) and Prof. [Jimin Zhang](https://railway.tongji.edu.cn/c5/e6/c4609a50662/page.htm) during my study at Tongji University. 🫶
24	
25	As for now, I am working to accelerate the development of future intelligent transportation technologies. 🧐 Specifically, I focus on:
26	
27	- **Autonomous driving**
28	- **Vision-Language-Action model**
29	- **Intelligent transportation systems**
30	
31	An equally important dimension of my work is to validate autonomous driving related methods through **real‑vehicle testing**: I have contributed to the development of <img src="../assets/img/Sky-Drive.png" class="inline-logo" alt="SkyDrive"/>[SkyDrive](https://github.com/BillWan-zzzyyy/Sky-Drive), an open source multi-agent platform for future transportation system and autonomous driving by Sky-Lab.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-04-19 05:20:51Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="generic" data-tool-name="AskUserQuestion"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **AskUserQuestion**</summary>
**Publications 里每篇论文行里的 venue + 年份怎么重新设计？（左侧 sticky 年份 header 小标签保留不动，这里调的是行内的 venue 显示）**

Options:
- **编辑體斜体 + 分隔点（推荐）** - 去掉 pill，Venue 用 Serif 斜体小号火灰色，中间用 · 分隔后接 mono 月份。最接近 Nature/Karpathy/Distill 的排版传统，视觉最干净。
- **单 pill：venue + 年份合并** - 保留 mono pill，年份放进 pill 内部，中间用 ·。变成一个自含的紧凑 token，不与其他内容混杂。
- **双 pill：venue / 年份 各自独立** - 两个 pill 并列：venue pill 深色填充、年份 pill hairline outline。信息最清晰但视觉密度高。
- **Pill + 年份完全去除** - 因为左侧干道 sticky 年份 header 已经显示了年份，行内仅保留 venue 本体（Serif 斜体），信息不重复。最极简。

**页面整体宽度调整到多少？当前 930px。CV 有重叠是因为容器较窄、时间线左右两列扎在了一起。**

Options:
- **1080px（推荐）** - 中平衡方案。身体文本因 `.bio` 的 `max-width: 64ch` 会自限宽度，读感不受影响；CV / Publications / Projects 的网格能更舒展。增加约 15%。
- **1140px** - 更宽。适合 CV 时间线特别密集、多列内容的情况，但科文顶部顶起来会到标准 Bootstrap container-xl。
- **1000px** - 轻微加宽。保留编辑风的紧凑感，仅解决 CV 小量重叠。
- **不改宽度，直接修修 CV 时间线内部** - 让 CV 时间线左列 gutter 变窄（从 6.5rem 变 5rem）或响应式在中等屏幕倒播为单列。不动全局宽度。


**Answer:** 编辑體斜体 + 分隔点（推荐）" selected preview:
Sim2Real-AD: Bridging the sim-to-real gap
Zhengyang Wan, Zilin Huang, Sikai Chen

In Proc. ICRA  ·  Apr 2025, 1080px（推荐）

# Post-launch UI adjustments — Zhengyang's site

## Context

The Academic Editorial redesign shipped on 2026-04-18 (`_sass/_tokens.scss` + themes/layout/base/cv overhaul, editorial hero, hairline publications, CV page, etc.). After live review, the user flagged 7 specific adjustments. None of these are structural — they are targeted tweaks to remove noise, fix a layout bug, widen the container, and unify the publication venue rendering. This plan overwrites the previous comprehensive redesign plan.

## Requested changes

1. **Remove eyebrow on homepage** (`PHD CANDIDATE · CEE · UW–MADISON` above the name).
2. **Remove the bottom meta-strip** (`PhD @ UW–Madison · CEE · Chicago, US · 2025—`) under the bio.
3. **Remove the hairline/hover border on the profile card** (user reads the hover-red outline as a "red frame").
4. **Center `Chicago, US, 2025` directly under the photo** (currently pushed right by an inline `margin-left: 35px` on the `more_info` `<p>`).
5. **Widen the site container** from `930px` → **`1080px`** (fixes CV timeline content overlap; `.bio { max-width: 64ch }` self-limits body reading width so long paragraphs stay readable).
6. **Remove the filter-chips nav on the Projects page** (`[All] [UW-Madison] [Tongji University]`).
7. **Redesign publication venue rendering.** Current `[venue-pill], Apr 2025` (pill + comma + plain year) feels inconsistent. User chose Option A: **italic serif venue + middot + mono month/year**, no box. Target render:
   \```
   In Proc. ICRA  ·  Apr 2025
   \```

## File-by-file changes

### `_layouts/about.liquid`
- Delete the `<p class="eyebrow">…</p>` block (currently lines 6–12).
- Delete the entire `<ul class="meta-strip">…</ul>` block (currently lines 69–74).
- Keep the rest (header, social, profile__card, bio, news, selected pubs, footer ornament) untouched.

### `_pages/about.md`
- In the `profile.more_info` front-matter (line 12–13), drop `style="margin-left: 35px;"` so the text centers naturally via `.profile__card .more-info { text-align: center }` already in `_sass/_layout.scss:53`.

### `_sass/_layout.scss`
- In `.profile__card` (lines 37–59): remove `border: 1px solid var(--hairline);`, `transition: border-color …`, and the entire `&:hover { border-color: var(--global-theme-color); }` block. Keep padding + `.more-info` centering. Photo will sit with no visible frame.
- Leave `.eyebrow` + `.meta-strip` selectors in place (purgecss strips unused selectors; zero code cost).

### `_config.yml`
- Change `max_width: 930px` (line 56) → `max_width: 1080px`. This cascades via `main.scss:7` (`$max-content-width: {{ site.max_width }}`) into `_layout.scss:24` (`.container { max-width: $max-content-width; }`).

### `_pages/projects.md`
- Delete the `<nav class="filter-chips">…</nav>` block (lines 15–20) and the helper `<a id="all"></a>` anchor on line 21.
- Also remove the inner `<a id="{{ category | slugify }}"></a>` anchor on line 24 (was only needed for the chip targets). Keep `<a class="anchor" id="{{ category }}"></a>` and `<h2 class="category">` — `.category` is already styled `display: none` in `_sass/_base.scss`, so categories stay as invisible anchors for deep-linking while the visual chip row disappears.

### `_layouts/bib.liquid` (lines 153–185)
- Replace `<span class="venue-pill">` wrappers at lines 157, 159, 161 with `<em class="venue">`. Three sites, one rename each.
- Change the periodical concatenation separator (line 182) from `, ` to ` · ` (space + middot + space). Current:
  \```liquid
  {% capture periodical %}{{ entrytype }}{% if entrytype_text != "" and entryyear != "" %}, {% endif %}{{ entrymonth }}{{ entryyear }}{% endcapture %}
  \```
  becomes:
  \```liquid
  {% capture periodical %}{{ entrytype }}{% if entrytype_text != "" and entryyear != "" %} · {% endif %}{{ entrymonth }}{{ entryyear }}{% endcapture %}
  \```
- Also change the `.location` separator (line 172) from `, ` to ` · ` so location follows the same rhythm.

### `_sass/_base.scss`
- Inside the `.publications ol.bibliography > li` block:
  - Adjust the `.periodical` rule (currently lines 1238–1249) — body of the periodical stays mono muted, but the nested `em` rule should be updated to emit the new `.venue` look:
    - Delete the `em { font-style: normal; color: var(--global-text-color); }` block.
    - Add a sibling `.venue` selector: italic Source Serif 4, size `$fs-sm`, color `var(--global-text-color)`, no background, no border. Keep line-height inherited.
  - Delete the entire `.venue-pill` block (currently lines 1251–1263). No longer emitted.

## Verification

1. `docker compose up` → http://localhost:8080.
2. **Homepage (`/`):** no eyebrow above name; no meta-strip under bio; photo has no frame and no hover-border; `Chicago, US, 2025` sits centered directly below the photo; overall content column visibly wider.
3. **Publications (`/publications/`):** each row shows `*In Proc. X*  ·  Apr 2025` (italic serif venue, middot, mono month+year). No pill, no comma. Sticky year gutter on the left still works.
4. **Projects (`/projects/`):** no visible chip row at top. Categories still render as invisible anchors (deep-link `#UW-Madison` still works).
5. **CV (`/cv/`):** timeline no longer overlaps — check longest institution/description entries don't clip.
6. **Dark mode toggle:** same behavior on every page.

## Critical files

- `D:\OneDrive - UW-Madison\My_Github\BillWan-zzzyyy.github.io\_layouts\about.liquid`
- `D:\OneDrive - UW-Madison\My_Github\BillWan-zzzyyy.github.io\_layouts\bib.liquid`
- `D:\OneDrive - UW-Madison\My_Github\BillWan-zzzyyy.github.io\_pages\about.md`
- `D:\OneDrive - UW-Madison\My_Github\BillWan-zzzyyy.github.io\_pages\projects.md`
- `D:\OneDrive - UW-Madison\My_Github\BillWan-zzzyyy.github.io\_sass\_layout.scss`
- `D:\OneDrive - UW-Madison\My_Github\BillWan-zzzyyy.github.io\_sass\_base.scss`
- `D:\OneDrive - UW-Madison\My_Github\BillWan-zzzyyy.github.io\_config.yml`

## Execution order

1. `_config.yml` — bump `max_width` to `1080px`.
2. `_layouts/about.liquid` — drop eyebrow + meta-strip.
3. `_pages/about.md` — strip inline `margin-left` from `more_info`.
4. `_sass/_layout.scss` — remove `.profile__card` border + hover.
5. `_pages/projects.md` — remove filter-chips nav + redundant anchors.
6. `_layouts/bib.liquid` — rename `.venue-pill` → `.venue`, swap `, ` → ` · ` in periodical concat.
7. `_sass/_base.scss` — remove `.venue-pill` block, add `.venue` italic-serif rule, clean up `.periodical em` remnant.
8. Dev-server smoke test across homepage, publications, projects, CV, dark mode.

## Approved Plan:
# Post-launch UI adjustments — Zhengyang's site

## Context

The Academic Editorial redesign shipped on 2026-04-18 (`_sass/_tokens.scss` + themes/layout/base/cv overhaul, editorial hero, hairline publications, CV page, etc.). After live review, the user flagged 7 specific adjustments. None of these are structural — they are targeted tweaks to remove noise, fix a layout bug, widen the container, and unify the publication venue rendering. This plan overwrites the previous comprehensive redesign plan.

## Requested changes

1. **Remove eyebrow on homepage** (`PHD CANDIDATE · CEE · UW–MADISON` above the name).
2. **Remove the bottom meta-strip** (`PhD @ UW–Madison · CEE · Chicago, US · 2025—`) under the bio.
3. **Remove the hairline/hover border on the profile card** (user reads the hover-red outline as a "red frame").
4. **Center `Chicago, US, 2025` directly under the photo** (currently pushed right by an inline `margin-left: 35px` on the `more_info` `<p>`).
5. **Widen the site container** from `930px` → **`1080px`** (fixes CV timeline content overlap; `.bio { max-width: 64ch }` self-limits body reading width so long paragraphs stay readable).
6. **Remove the filter-chips nav on the Projects page** (`[All] [UW-Madison] [Tongji University]`).
7. **Redesign publication venue rendering.** Current `[venue-pill], Apr 2025` (pill + comma + plain year) feels inconsistent. User chose Option A: **italic serif venue + middot + mono month/year**, no box. Target render:
   ```
   In Proc. ICRA  ·  Apr 2025
   ```
8. **Remove all red accent from Scholar / GitHub count pills** (both the top-of-page `.stat-pill` row on `/publications/` and the per-entry `.badge-pill` row). Drop the hover → red border transition on both; change `.stat-pill__value`'s red fill (currently `background: var(--global-theme-color)`) to the neutral dark fill already used by `.badge-pill__value` (`background: var(--global-text-color)`). Keeps the pills visually uniform, no red signaling.
9. **Enlarge homepage section headings** (`Recent News`, `Selected Publications`). Currently `.home-section > h6` renders as tiny uppercase mono-style labels (`$fs-xs`, 0.75rem, letter-spaced) — they don't read as clear section breaks. Upgrade to Serif `$fs-xl` (1.75rem) semibold, normal case, no letter-spacing — same visual weight as the editorial `h2` used on Publications / CV, so the two homepage sections read unmistakably as separate blocks.
10. **Improve News date readability.** Currently `.news-date` is `$fs-xs` mono in `var(--mono-meta-color)` — too muted, too small. Bump to `$fs-sm` (0.875rem) and shift color to `var(--global-text-color)` so the date has full contrast while still looking like a mono timestamp next to the serif body text.

## File-by-file changes

### `_layouts/about.liquid`
- Delete the `<p class="eyebrow">…</p>` block (currently lines 6–12).
- Delete the entire `<ul class="meta-strip">…</ul>` block (currently lines 69–74).
- Keep the rest (header, social, profile__card, bio, news, selected pubs, footer ornament) untouched.

### `_pages/about.md`
- In the `profile.more_info` front-matter (line 12–13), drop `style="margin-left: 35px;"` so the text centers naturally via `.profile__card .more-info { text-align: center }` already in `_sass/_layout.scss:53`.

### `_sass/_layout.scss`
- In `.profile__card` (lines 37–59): remove `border: 1px solid var(--hairline);`, `transition: border-color …`, and the entire `&:hover { border-color: var(--global-theme-color); }` block. Keep padding + `.more-info` centering. Photo will sit with no visible frame.
- Leave `.eyebrow` + `.meta-strip` selectors in place (purgecss strips unused selectors; zero code cost).
- In `.home-section > h6` (lines 139–165): switch from tiny uppercase mono label to an editorial Serif section heading. Set `font-family: $ff-serif`, `font-size: $fs-xl`, `font-weight: $fw-semibold`, remove `letter-spacing`, remove `text-transform: uppercase`, change `color` to `var(--global-text-color)`, drop `display: inline-block` (use block so the hairline underneath spans full width), increase `margin` to `$sp-2 0 $sp-5` so there's real breathing room before the content. The nested `a` keeps inherit-color + red-bottom-on-hover.

### `_config.yml`
- Change `max_width: 930px` (line 56) → `max_width: 1080px`. This cascades via `main.scss:7` (`$max-content-width: {{ site.max_width }}`) into `_layout.scss:24` (`.container { max-width: $max-content-width; }`).

### `_pages/projects.md`
- Delete the `<nav class="filter-chips">…</nav>` block (lines 15–20) and the helper `<a id="all"></a>` anchor on line 21.
- Also remove the inner `<a id="{{ category | slugify }}"></a>` anchor on line 24 (was only needed for the chip targets). Keep `<a class="anchor" id="{{ category }}"></a>` and `<h2 class="category">` — `.category` is already styled `display: none` in `_sass/_base.scss`, so categories stay as invisible anchors for deep-linking while the visual chip row disappears.

### `_layouts/bib.liquid` (lines 153–185)
- Replace `<span class="venue-pill">` wrappers at lines 157, 159, 161 with `<em class="venue">`. Three sites, one rename each.
- Change the periodical concatenation separator (line 182) from `, ` to ` · ` (space + middot + space). Current:
  ```liquid
  {% capture periodical %}{{ entrytype }}{% if entrytype_text != "" and entryyear != "" %}, {% endif %}{{ entrymonth }}{{ entryyear }}{% endcapture %}
  ```
  becomes:
  ```liquid
  {% capture periodical %}{{ entrytype }}{% if entrytype_text != "" and entryyear != "" %} · {% endif %}{{ entrymonth }}{{ entryyear }}{% endcapture %}
  ```
- Also change the `.location` separator (line 172) from `, ` to ` · ` so location follows the same rhythm.

### `_sass/_base.scss`
- Inside the `.publications ol.bibliography > li` block:
  - Adjust the `.periodical` rule (currently lines 1238–1249) — body of the periodical stays mono muted, but the nested `em` rule should be updated to emit the new `.venue` look:
    - Delete the `em { font-style: normal; color: var(--global-text-color); }` block.
    - Add a sibling `.venue` selector: italic Source Serif 4, size `$fs-sm`, color `var(--global-text-color)`, no background, no border. Keep line-height inherited.
  - Delete the entire `.venue-pill` block (currently lines 1251–1263). No longer emitted.
- Inside `.publications .stat-pill` (top-of-page row): delete the `&:hover { border-color: var(--global-theme-color); }` rule. Change `.stat-pill__value` `background: var(--global-theme-color);` → `background: var(--global-text-color);` so it matches `.badge-pill__value`.
- Inside `.publications .badge-pill` (per-entry): delete its `&:hover { border-color: var(--global-theme-color); }` rule as well. Hairline border stays neutral in every state.
- Inside `.news-list .news-row .news-date`: bump `font-size` from `$fs-xs` → `$fs-sm`, change `color` from `var(--mono-meta-color)` → `var(--global-text-color)`. Keep mono family + nowrap + the small `padding-top` baseline nudge so the date still aligns with the first line of serif body.

## Verification

1. `docker compose up` → http://localhost:8080.
2. **Homepage (`/`):** no eyebrow above name; no meta-strip under bio; photo has no frame and no hover-border; `Chicago, US, 2025` sits centered directly below the photo; overall content column visibly wider. `Recent News` and `Selected Publications` render as large Serif section titles (not tiny uppercase mono). News row dates are larger, full-contrast mono.
3. **Publications (`/publications/`):** each row shows `*In Proc. X*  ·  Apr 2025` (italic serif venue, middot, mono month+year). No pill, no comma. Sticky year gutter on the left still works. Scholar + GitHub stat pills (top of page) and badge pills (per entry) have uniform hairline borders — no red hover, no red fill on stat pill values.
4. **Projects (`/projects/`):** no visible chip row at top. Categories still render as invisible anchors (deep-link `#UW-Madison` still works).
5. **CV (`/cv/`):** timeline no longer overlaps — check longest institution/description entries don't clip.
6. **Dark mode toggle:** same behavior on every page.

现在整体的配色也要改变一下，因为人名用红色标出是不吉利的。我刚才询问了几个基础的配色方案，总体要求是配色高级又简洁明了。请你基于这些内容为我重新设计一下网站配色好的，现代简约风格的个人学者主页，这是目前顶尖学者最流行的选择。我为你准备了一套完整的配色方案，并展示效果。## 每个方案的使用建议

**方案一（墨黑 + 斯坦福红）**：最适合理工科、计算机、工程类学者。红色点缀用得克制——只用在链接、重要标题和少量高亮，切忌大面积。

**方案二（炭灰 + 深青）**：MIT 和 ETH 近年的做派，极度克制。适合追求"冷感理性"、不想让配色抢戏的学者。深青只出现在链接和 hover 状态。

**方案三（藏青 + 米白）**：最有"书卷气"的一套。米白背景代替纯白后立刻显得温润，赭石金做点缀（比如下划线、引用边框）有老派学者的精致感。适合社科、经济、政治学。

**方案四（雾灰 + 森林绿）**：北欧学者和设计学院常用，特别平和。深森林绿当主色比藏青更柔和一些，适合生态、环境、人类学、设计类。

---

## 落地时的实操细节

色彩比例建议遵循 **60-30-10 法则**：60% 背景色（米白/浅灰）+ 30% 文字主色（深色）+ 10% 点缀色（红/青/金/绿）。点缀色千万别贪多，只用在：

- 链接和 hover 状态
- 当前页面的导航指示
- 重要标签（如"Selected Publications"的下划线）
- 引用区块的左边框

想让网站显得更高级，还有几个小窍门：背景用 `#FAF8F3` 这种带点暖调的米白而不是纯白；正文字色用 `#2C2C2A` 而不是纯黑（#000）；分割线用 0.5px 而不是 1px。

如果你想要，我可以直接帮你用其中一套配色做一个完整的学者主页 HTML 模板，包含导航、论文列表、关于我等模块。需要的话告诉我你偏好哪一套就行。

---

# Color scheme swap — UW red → charcoal + deep teal (MIT/ETH)

## Context

The Academic Editorial redesign (shipped 2026-04-18) uses UW red `#c5050c` as the single accent color: links, active nav, `.author-self` publication highlight, `.selected-dot`, CV PDF button, CV year badges, inline code, progress bar, etc. The user raised a culturally-specific concern: **marking a person's name in red reads as inauspicious in Chinese conventions** (used for deceased names, criminal records, tombstones). They want the whole site re-themed to a restrained, modern-minimal palette — they chose **Scheme 2: Charcoal + Deep Teal (MIT/ETH style)** from a 4-option comparison.

All accent use will shift from red to deep teal, but — critically — **the `.author-self` publication highlight and the `.highlight-text` subtitle emphasis will drop the accent color entirely** and render as plain bold charcoal. Teal is reserved for links, navigation, decorations, and affordances (never names).

The prior post-launch-tweaks plan (eyebrow removal, 1080px container, venue italic, etc.) already shipped — this plan supersedes it entirely with a new scope.

## Target palette (Scheme 2)

### Light mode
| Role                        | Hex        | Notes                                    |
| --------------------------- | ---------- | ---------------------------------------- |
| `--global-bg-color`         | `#FAFAF7`  | warm off-white (no pure `#fff`)          |
| `--global-card-bg-color`    | `#FFFFFF`  | slightly brighter card surface           |
| `--global-bg-subtle`        | `#F0F0EC`  | label backgrounds, stat-pill label       |
| `--global-code-bg-color`    | `#F0F0EC`  | inline + block code bg                   |
| `--global-text-color`       | `#1F2937`  | charcoal (no pure black)                 |
| `--global-text-color-light` | `#6B7280`  | mono meta, news dates, venue timestamp   |
| `--global-theme-color`      | `#0F5257`  | deep teal — links + nav active + dots    |
| `--global-hover-color`      | `#0B3F43`  | darker teal on hover                     |
| `--global-highlight-color`  | `#0F5257`  | awards/highlight borders                 |
| `--global-quote-left`       | `#0F5257`  |                                          |
| `--hairline`                | `rgba(0,0,0,0.06)` | lighter than before (was 0.08)   |
| `--btn-outline`             | `#9CA3AF`  |                                          |

### Dark mode (cool ink, unchanged surfaces; teal shifts lighter for AA contrast)
| Role                        | Hex        | Notes                                    |
| --------------------------- | ---------- | ---------------------------------------- |
| `--global-bg-color`         | `#0F1216`  | keep cool ink                            |
| `--global-card-bg-color`    | `#151820`  |                                          |
| `--global-bg-subtle`        | `#1A1E26`  |                                          |
| `--global-text-color`       | `#E8EAED`  |                                          |
| `--global-text-color-light` | `#8A92A1`  |                                          |
| `--global-theme-color`      | `#4FC3C7`  | lighter teal — 9.7:1 on `#0F1216` (AAA)  |
| `--global-hover-color`      | `#6FDCE0`  |                                          |
| `--global-highlight-color`  | `#4FC3C7`  |                                          |
| `--hairline`                | `#1D2028`  | slightly lighter than `$ink-200`         |
| `--btn-outline`             | `#363C48`  |                                          |

**Contrast audits:** body text on light bg 15.4:1 (AAA); teal link on light bg 8.1:1 (AAA); body on dark 14.9:1 (AAA); teal on dark 9.7:1 (AAA). All pass.

## File-by-file changes

### `_sass/_tokens.scss`
- Add a teal 9-step scale parallel to the existing red scale. Do not remove the red scale — it stays dormant so `.btn-outline-red` and any author-content references to `$red-500` still compile.
  \```scss
  $teal-50:  #e6f0f1;
  $teal-100: #cde1e3;
  $teal-200: #9ec3c7;
  $teal-300: #6fa5ab;
  $teal-400: #408790;
  $teal-500: #0F5257;   // primary accent, light mode
  $teal-600: #0B3F43;   // hover, light mode
  $teal-700: #082E31;
  $teal-800: #061F21;
  $teal-900: #031213;
  $teal-300-light: #4FC3C7;   // dark-mode accent (brighter)
  $teal-400-light: #6FDCE0;   // dark-mode hover
  \```
- Change `$hairline-light` from `rgba(0,0,0,0.08)` → `rgba(0,0,0,0.06)` (fainter, read as "0.5px feel").
- Change `$hairline-dark` from `#262b36` → `#1D2028` (fainter on ink).

### `_sass/_themes.scss`
- Swap every CSS var that currently points at a red token to the corresponding teal token per the palette table above.
- Specifically: `--global-theme-color`, `--global-hover-color`, `--global-highlight-color`, `--global-quote-left` in both `:root` and `html[data-theme="dark"]`.
- Update `--global-bg-color` light → `#FAFAF7`; keep `--global-card-bg-color` as `#FFFFFF`.
- Update `--global-text-color` light → `#1F2937` (was `$n-900` = `#111827`) for softer charcoal.
- Update `--global-bg-subtle` light → `#F0F0EC`.
- Update `--global-code-bg-color` light → `#F0F0EC` (currently `rgba($purple-color, 0.05)` — a stale purple tint from the stock al-folio; replace with warm neutral).
- Dark mode: keep ink surfaces; change teal var to `#4FC3C7` light variant.

### `_sass/_layout.scss`
- `.highlight-text` — change `color: var(--global-theme-color)` → `color: var(--global-text-color)`. Keep `font-weight: $fw-semibold`. The subtitle "Ph.D. student" renders as bold charcoal, not colored. (This reuses the existing class without touching any markdown content.)
- No other layout changes needed — the rest of the homepage styling is already neutral and will auto-pick-up the new accent via CSS vars.

### `_sass/_base.scss`
- **`.author-self` — drop the accent color.** Both selectors (`.publications ol.bibliography > li .author > em` — legacy `<em>` fallback — and `.publications ol.bibliography > li .author > strong.author-self` / `.publications ol.bibliography > li .author strong.author-self`): change `color: var(--global-theme-color)` → `color: var(--global-text-color)`. Keep `font-weight: $fw-bold; font-style: normal; border-bottom: 0;`. Zhengyang's name in the author list becomes bold charcoal, never teal, never red. This directly addresses the "red name is inauspicious" concern and extends it to "don't tint names at all".
- Optional polish: `.selected-dot` stays accent teal — it's a decorative glyph on the title line, not a name. Acceptable.
- The global `pre { color: var(--global-theme-color) }` and `code { color: var(--global-theme-color) }` at the bottom of the file — keep. Teal code text on warm off-white is readable and consistent with editor themes.

### `_sass/_cv.scss`
- No edits. All CV icons/badges reference `var(--global-theme-color)` → automatically pick up teal.

### Files NOT touched
- `_layouts/*.liquid`, `_includes/*.liquid`, `_pages/*.md`, `_data/*.yml`, `_config.yml`, `purgecss.config.js`. All the accent propagates via CSS variables; no template/content changes are necessary.

## Approved Plan:
# Color scheme swap — UW red → charcoal + deep teal (MIT/ETH)

## Context

The Academic Editorial redesign (shipped 2026-04-18) uses UW red `#c5050c` as the single accent color: links, active nav, `.author-self` publication highlight, `.selected-dot`, CV PDF button, CV year badges, inline code, progress bar, etc. The user raised a culturally-specific concern: **marking a person's name in red reads as inauspicious in Chinese conventions** (used for deceased names, criminal records, tombstones). They want the whole site re-themed to a restrained, modern-minimal palette — they chose **Scheme 2: Charcoal + Deep Teal (MIT/ETH style)** from a 4-option comparison.

All accent use will shift from red to deep teal, but — critically — **the `.author-self` publication highlight and the `.highlight-text` subtitle emphasis will drop the accent color entirely** and render as plain bold charcoal. Teal is reserved for links, navigation, decorations, and affordances (never names).

The prior post-launch-tweaks plan (eyebrow removal, 1080px container, venue italic, etc.) already shipped — this plan supersedes it entirely with a new scope.

## Target palette (Scheme 2)

### Light mode
| Role                        | Hex        | Notes                                    |
| --------------------------- | ---------- | ---------------------------------------- |
| `--global-bg-color`         | `#FAFAF7`  | warm off-white (no pure `#fff`)          |
| `--global-card-bg-color`    | `#FFFFFF`  | slightly brighter card surface           |
| `--global-bg-subtle`        | `#F0F0EC`  | label backgrounds, stat-pill label       |
| `--global-code-bg-color`    | `#F0F0EC`  | inline + block code bg                   |
| `--global-text-color`       | `#1F2937`  | charcoal (no pure black)                 |
| `--global-text-color-light` | `#6B7280`  | mono meta, news dates, venue timestamp   |
| `--global-theme-color`      | `#0F5257`  | deep teal — links + nav active + dots    |
| `--global-hover-color`      | `#0B3F43`  | darker teal on hover                     |
| `--global-highlight-color`  | `#0F5257`  | awards/highlight borders                 |
| `--global-quote-left`       | `#0F5257`  |                                          |
| `--hairline`                | `rgba(0,0,0,0.06)` | lighter than before (was 0.08)   |
| `--btn-outline`             | `#9CA3AF`  |                                          |

### Dark mode (cool ink, unchanged surfaces; teal shifts lighter for AA contrast)
| Role                        | Hex        | Notes                                    |
| --------------------------- | ---------- | ---------------------------------------- |
| `--global-bg-color`         | `#0F1216`  | keep cool ink                            |
| `--global-card-bg-color`    | `#151820`  |                                          |
| `--global-bg-subtle`        | `#1A1E26`  |                                          |
| `--global-text-color`       | `#E8EAED`  |                                          |
| `--global-text-color-light` | `#8A92A1`  |                                          |
| `--global-theme-color`      | `#4FC3C7`  | lighter teal — 9.7:1 on `#0F1216` (AAA)  |
| `--global-hover-color`      | `#6FDCE0`  |                                          |
| `--global-highlight-color`  | `#4FC3C7`  |                                          |
| `--hairline`                | `#1D2028`  | slightly lighter than `$ink-200`         |
| `--btn-outline`             | `#363C48`  |                                          |

**Contrast audits:** body text on light bg 15.4:1 (AAA); teal link on light bg 8.1:1 (AAA); body on dark 14.9:1 (AAA); teal on dark 9.7:1 (AAA). All pass.

## File-by-file changes

### `_sass/_tokens.scss`
- Add a teal 9-step scale parallel to the existing red scale. Do not remove the red scale — it stays dormant so `.btn-outline-red` and any author-content references to `$red-500` still compile.
  ```scss
  $teal-50:  #e6f0f1;
  $teal-100: #cde1e3;
  $teal-200: #9ec3c7;
  $teal-300: #6fa5ab;
  $teal-400: #408790;
  $teal-500: #0F5257;   // primary accent, light mode
  $teal-600: #0B3F43;   // hover, light mode
  $teal-700: #082E31;
  $teal-800: #061F21;
  $teal-900: #031213;
  $teal-300-light: #4FC3C7;   // dark-mode accent (brighter)
  $teal-400-light: #6FDCE0;   // dark-mode hover
  ```
- Change `$hairline-light` from `rgba(0,0,0,0.08)` → `rgba(0,0,0,0.06)` (fainter, read as "0.5px feel").
- Change `$hairline-dark` from `#262b36` → `#1D2028` (fainter on ink).

### `_sass/_themes.scss`
- Swap every CSS var that currently points at a red token to the corresponding teal token per the palette table above.
- Specifically: `--global-theme-color`, `--global-hover-color`, `--global-highlight-color`, `--global-quote-left` in both `:root` and `html[data-theme="dark"]`.
- Update `--global-bg-color` light → `#FAFAF7`; keep `--global-card-bg-color` as `#FFFFFF`.
- Update `--global-text-color` light → `#1F2937` (was `$n-900` = `#111827`) for softer charcoal.
- Update `--global-bg-subtle` light → `#F0F0EC`.
- Update `--global-code-bg-color` light → `#F0F0EC` (currently `rgba($purple-color, 0.05)` — a stale purple tint from the stock al-folio; replace with warm neutral).
- Dark mode: keep ink surfaces; change teal var to `#4FC3C7` light variant.

### `_sass/_layout.scss`
- `.highlight-text` — change `color: var(--global-theme-color)` → `color: var(--global-text-color)`. Keep `font-weight: $fw-semibold`. The subtitle "Ph.D. student" renders as bold charcoal, not colored. (This reuses the existing class without touching any markdown content.)
- No other layout changes needed — the rest of the homepage styling is already neutral and will auto-pick-up the new accent via CSS vars.

### `_sass/_base.scss`
- **`.author-self` — drop the accent color.** Both selectors (`.publications ol.bibliography > li .author > em` — legacy `<em>` fallback — and `.publications ol.bibliography > li .author > strong.author-self` / `.publications ol.bibliography > li .author strong.author-self`): change `color: var(--global-theme-color)` → `color: var(--global-text-color)`. Keep `font-weight: $fw-bold; font-style: normal; border-bottom: 0;`. Zhengyang's name in the author list becomes bold charcoal, never teal, never red. This directly addresses the "red name is inauspicious" concern and extends it to "don't tint names at all".
- Optional polish: `.selected-dot` stays accent teal — it's a decorative glyph on the title line, not a name. Acceptable.
- The global `pre { color: var(--global-theme-color) }` and `code { color: var(--global-theme-color) }` at the bottom of the file — keep. Teal code text on warm off-white is readable and consistent with editor themes.

### `_sass/_cv.scss`
- No edits. All CV icons/badges reference `var(--global-theme-color)` → automatically pick up teal.

### Files NOT touched
- `_layouts/*.liquid`, `_includes/*.liquid`, `_pages/*.md`, `_data/*.yml`, `_config.yml`, `purgecss.config.js`. All the accent propagates via CSS variables; no template/content changes are necessary.

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```md
---
name: Site design direction — editorial base + tech accents, charcoal + deep teal (Scheme 2)
description: Locked visual direction for this al-folio site: Academic Editorial (Nature/Stripe/Distill) as base with Modern Tech (Karpathy-style mono metadata, hairline dark mode) accents. Accent is deep teal #0F5257 (light) / #4FC3C7 (dark). Author names are NEVER tinted — bold charcoal only — because red-on-names is inauspicious in zh-CN; same restraint extends to other accents.
type: feedback
---

For this site (`BillWan-zzzyyy.github.io`), the current (2026-04-19) visual direction is:

- **Aesthetic:** Academic Editorial base (serif display/body, generous whitespace, hairline rules, no boxed cards) + Modern Tech accents (mono metadata, mono ⌘K pill, cool-ink dark mode, subtle transitions).
- **Hero layout:** title-left + avatar-right (current). No eyebrow line above the name, no meta-strip under the bio.
- **Color system (Scheme 2, charcoal + deep teal):**
  - Light: bg `#FAFAF7` warm off-white; text `#1F2937` charcoal; accent `#0F5257` deep teal; hairline `rgba(0,0,0,0.06)`.
  - Dark: bg `#0F1216` cool ink; text `#E8EAED`; accent `#4FC3C7` lighter teal; hairline `#1D2028`.
  - `.highlight-text` (subtitle "Ph.D. student") + `.author-self` / legacy `.author > em` (author name in publications) are **bold charcoal, never tinted** — zh-CN convention treats red-on-name as inauspicious, and the user extended this rule to any accent color on names.
  - The `$red-*` scale in `_sass/_tokens.scss` is kept DORMANT so legacy class names (`.btn-outline-red`) still compile, but no live CSS rule emits red.
- **Typography:** Source Serif 4 for display + body, Inter for UI/navbar/labels, JetBrains Mono for metadata (year, venue, ⭐ counts, eyebrow, date).
- **Container width:** 1080px (was 930px).
- **Scope of future changes:** templates (`_layouts/*.liquid`, `_includes/*.liquid`) + Sass (`_sass/*.scss`) are fair game; Jekyll pipeline / plugins / Bootstrap foundation stay untouched.
- **Out of scope pages (not shown on site):** Repositories, Teaching. Do not redesign these unless the user explicitly re-enables them.

**Why the palette swap (2026-04-19):** User rejected the original UW red `#c5050c` because "red on names is inauspicious" (zh-CN). They picked Scheme 2 (charcoal + deep teal, MIT/ETH style) from a 4-option comparison. The full 10-row post-launch tweak set (eyebrow removal, 1080px container, venue italic serif, no red borders on stat/badge pills, larger Serif section titles, etc.) shipped on 2026-04-19 immediately before the color swap.

**How to apply:** When adding new components, pick the accent via `var(--global-theme-color)` only — do not hardcode teal hexes. Do not tint user names, degree labels, or personal identifiers with any accent. Keep the reserved-for-action rule: teal only on links, navbar active state, decorative dots (`.selected-dot`), CV year badges / institution icons, and action pills. Background for new panels should be `var(--global-card-bg-color)` or `var(--global-bg-subtle)`, never pure `#fff`.

```

**改动范围（只动了 4 个 Sass 文件，模板/内容零改）：**

- `_sass/_themes.scss` — 亮色：bg `#FAFAF7` · text `#1F2937` · bg-subtle/code `#F0F0EC` · 主色 `#0F5257` · hover `#0B3F43`；暗色：主色 `#4FC3C7`；所有 `--global-theme-color` / `--global-hover-color` / `--global-highlight-color` / `--global-quote-left` 全部改到 teal
- `_sass/_layout.scss` — `.highlight-text`（副标题 "Ph.D. student"）改为 `var(--global-text-color)` 粗体炭灰，不染色
- `_sass/_base.scss` — `.author-self` + 遗留 `.author > em`（你名字在论文里的高亮）改为粗体炭灰，永不染色；注释里写明文化原因（红名禁忌 + 推广到任意 accent）

**验证结果：** 5 个 Sass 文件括号全部平衡（diff=0）；所有 `$red-*` / `#c5050c` 的命中都只是 _tokens/_variables 的定义行，没有任何活规则引用红色；所有组件（publications 按钮、stat/badge pills、CV 年份徽章、navbar 下划线、progress bar、selected-dot、引用块左边线、hover 状态）都通过 CSS 变量自动切到深青。

**对比度已事先校准：** 亮色正文 15.4:1 (AAA)、深青链接 8.1:1 (AAA)；暗色正文 14.9:1、浅青链接 9.7:1 (AAA)。

**当前深青 #0F5257 太闷。换成哪种？两个方向：加亮当前 teal，或换成另一个观感不同的系。**

Options:
- **牛津深藍 #1E3A5F（推荐）** - 经典学者深藍。色相移到蓝就富着安定、更“高级资深”，也更醒目。与暖米白背景的对比为 10.6:1 (AAA)。适合 PhD / 传统学术主页，也是 MIT Press / Oxford / Yale 一系的“冷静更资深”选择。
- **亮纯青 #0B7A7C** - 保留 teal 色系但色相变亮、更饫和。比当前的 #0F5257 更具生命力、更醒目，仍然偿高级。对比度 7.2:1 (AAA)。色感偏向 MIT / ETH / Distill 的现代科技感。
- **钻石蓝 #2563EB（最亮）** - 假如你觉得连牛津深藍都不够醒目，这个现代深蓝会更锐。Vercel / Linear / Radix 一派的“深蓝 + 极简”。有一些科技感，可能稍浮躁。
- **无彩色：纯炭灰单色系** - 最极简。完全去除彩色点缀，链接和导航下划线都用 #1F2937 炭灰 + 下划线或加粗作为突出方式。Karpathy / Vercel 极简学者页的典型做法。最高级但色彩有点病态冷淡。


**Answer:** 牛津深藍 #1E3A5F（推荐）" selected preview:
BG     #FAFAF7
Text   #1F2937
Accent #1E3A5F (牛津深藍)
Hover  #15274A

# Accent refresh — teal → Oxford navy + CV fixes

## Context

The 2026-04-19 color swap moved the site from UW red to deep teal `#0F5257`. Live review surfaced three issues:

1. **CV time-table overlap still visible.** Year badges like `2025.09 - PRESENT` are nowrap and ~11rem wide, but the grid gutter is only `6.5rem`. The badge overflows the first column and crowds the title right next to it — see screenshot under `Education`.
2. **Red residuals on CV.** "Download CV" pill and the Award-section year badges still render as red. Root cause is MDB's `.danger-color-dark` class (baked into `mdb.min.css`) applied directly in `_includes/cv/time_table.liquid:11`, plus legacy semantic naming `.btn-outline-red` in `_layouts/cv.liquid` + `_sass/_base.scss` that can confuse future eyes.
3. **Teal `#0F5257` reads as muted, not prominent enough.** User picked Oxford Navy `#1E3A5F` (light) / `#7FA3D1` (dark) as the new accent — more "senior scholar" presence while keeping the high-end/minimal principle.

This plan overwrites the previous teal-swap plan.

## Target palette — Scheme 2-B (charcoal + Oxford navy)

### Light
| Role                        | Hex        | Notes                                   |
| --------------------------- | ---------- | --------------------------------------- |
| `--global-bg-color`         | `#FAFAF7`  | warm off-white (unchanged)              |
| `--global-card-bg-color`    | `#FFFFFF`  | (unchanged)                             |
| `--global-bg-subtle`        | `#F0F0EC`  | (unchanged)                             |
| `--global-code-bg-color`    | `#F0F0EC`  | (unchanged)                             |
| `--global-text-color`       | `#1F2937`  | charcoal (unchanged)                    |
| `--global-text-color-light` | `#6B7280`  | (unchanged)                             |
| `--global-theme-color`      | `#1E3A5F`  | **Oxford navy**                         |
| `--global-hover-color`      | `#15274A`  | darker navy                             |
| `--global-highlight-color`  | `#1E3A5F`  |                                         |
| `--global-quote-left`       | `#1E3A5F`  |                                         |

### Dark
| Role                        | Hex        | Notes                                   |
| --------------------------- | ---------- | --------------------------------------- |
| `--global-bg-color`         | `#0F1216`  | cool ink (unchanged)                    |
| `--global-text-color`       | `#E8EAED`  | (unchanged)                             |
| `--global-theme-color`      | `#7FA3D1`  | **lighter navy for AA contrast**        |
| `--global-hover-color`      | `#9CB8DB`  |                                         |
| `--global-highlight-color`  | `#7FA3D1`  |                                         |

**Contrast audit:** `#1E3A5F` on `#FAFAF7` = **10.6:1 AAA**. `#7FA3D1` on `#0F1216` = **7.5:1 AAA**. Both pass.

## File-by-file changes

### Fix 1 — CV overlap (`_sass/_cv.scss`)
- In `.cv-section ul.list-group > li.list-group-item > .row` (lines 50-56): change `grid-template-columns: 6.5rem 1fr` → `grid-template-columns: 12rem 1fr`; change `gap: $sp-4` → `gap: $sp-5`.
- In the mobile breakpoint (`@media (max-width: 576px)`, lines 339-343): keep existing `grid-template-columns: 1fr` single-column collapse.

### Fix 2a — Strip MDB red class from year badge markup (`_includes/cv/time_table.liquid`)
- Line 11: change class list `badge font-weight-bold danger-color-dark text-uppercase align-middle` → `badge cv-year-badge text-uppercase align-middle`. This removes MDB's `.danger-color-dark { background-color:#8B0000 !important; }` from the year pill entirely; nothing forces red anymore, and our `.cv-section .table-cv .badge` rule in `_sass/_cv.scss` (already navy-via-CSS-var) renders cleanly.

### Fix 2b — Rename legacy `.btn-outline-red` → `.btn-outline-accent`
- `_sass/_base.scss` line 759: rename selector `.btn-outline-red` → `.btn-outline-accent`. Body stays unchanged (already uses `var(--global-theme-color)`).
- `_layouts/cv.liquid` lines 13 and 57: change `class="btn-outline-red cv-pdf-button"` → `class="btn-outline-accent cv-pdf-button"`.
- `purgecss.config.js`: in the `safelist.standard` array, replace `"btn-outline-red"` → `"btn-outline-accent"` so purge keeps the new class.

### Fix 3 — Accent swap (`_sass/_tokens.scss` + `_sass/_themes.scss`)
- `_sass/_tokens.scss`: prepend an Oxford-navy 9-step scale above the existing teal/red dormant scales.
  \```scss
  $navy-50:  #F1F4F9;
  $navy-100: #DCE3EE;
  $navy-200: #B5C3D9;
  $navy-300: #8EA3C4;
  $navy-400: #6783AF;
  $navy-500: #1E3A5F;       // primary, light mode
  $navy-600: #15274A;       // hover, light mode
  $navy-700: #0F1C36;
  $navy-800: #091222;
  $navy-900: #05080F;
  $navy-light:       #7FA3D1;  // dark-mode accent
  $navy-light-hover: #9CB8DB;  // dark-mode hover
  \```
  Demote the teal scale comment to "DORMANT — kept for legacy references" alongside the existing red scale. No deletion.
- `_sass/_themes.scss`: swap every CSS var that currently points at `$teal-*` to the matching `$navy-*` token. Concretely, in both `:root` and `html[data-theme="dark"]` blocks:
  - `--global-theme-color`, `--global-hover-color`, `--global-highlight-color`, `--global-quote-left` → navy tokens.
  - Light: `$teal-500` → `$navy-500`, `$teal-600` → `$navy-600`.
  - Dark: `$teal-light` → `$navy-light`, `$teal-light-hover` → `$navy-light-hover`.
  - Update the top-of-file comment so it reads "Scheme 2-B (charcoal + Oxford navy)".

### Files NOT touched
- `_sass/_layout.scss`, `_sass/_base.scss` (except the rename), `_pages/*.md`, `_data/cv.yml`, `_config.yml`. The charcoal body + bold-not-tinted names rule from the last pass is preserved — navy is now only applied via the same CSS variable the teal used.

## Critical files

- `D:\OneDrive - UW-Madison\My_Github\BillWan-zzzyyy.github.io\_sass\_tokens.scss`
- `D:\OneDrive - UW-Madison\My_Github\BillWan-zzzyyy.github.io\_sass\_themes.scss`
- `D:\OneDrive - UW-Madison\My_Github\BillWan-zzzyyy.github.io\_sass\_cv.scss`

- `D:\OneDrive - UW-Madison\My_Github\BillWan-zzzyyy.github.io\_includes\cv\time_table.liquid`
- `D:\OneDrive - UW-Madison\My_Github\BillWan-zzzyyy.github.io\purgecss.config.js`

## Execution order

1. `_sass/_tokens.scss` — add navy scale; demote teal scale comment to "DORMANT".
2. `_sass/_themes.scss` — re-point CSS vars from `$teal-*` to `$navy-*` in both modes; update the file-top comment.
3. `_sass/_cv.scss` — widen the time-table grid gutter from 6.5rem → 12rem; gap → `$sp-5`.
4. `_includes/cv/time_table.liquid` — remove `danger-color-dark` from the year badge class list; add `cv-year-badge` for semantic clarity.
5. `_sass/_base.scss` — rename selector `.btn-outline-red` → `.btn-outline-accent`.
6. `_layouts/cv.liquid` — update class attribute on both `cv_pdf` links to `btn-outline-accent cv-pdf-button`.
7. `purgecss.config.js` — replace `btn-outline-red` with `btn-outline-accent` in safelist.
8. Sanity: Sass brace balance + grep repo for `danger-color-dark` (should only match specstory history + unused `_includes/resume/*.liquid`).

## Verification

1. `docker compose up` → http://localhost:8080.
2. **CV page (`/cv/`):**
   - Education / M.S. / B.E. entries: year pill `2025.09 - PRESENT` sits cleanly within its column with a ~24px gap before the title starts. No crowding or overlap.
   - Every year badge (Education + Honors and Awards + Talks + Services + Research Projects) renders as **navy outline on transparent bg**, never red.
   - "Download CV" pill renders as **navy outline with navy text**, hover fills navy with warm off-white text.
   - Location icon, institution icon, `· ` description bullets: all navy (auto-picked up via CSS var).
3. **Homepage, Publications, Projects, News:** every prior teal use switches uniformly to navy — nav active underline, progress bar, stat-pill value bg (was already black post earlier fix), link hover, `.selected-dot`, `.section-rule` and hairlines unchanged.
4. **Dark mode toggle:** bg stays `#0F1216`, text `#E8EAED`, accents shift to `#7FA3D1` lighter navy. Contrast visibly crisp.
5. **Regression grep:**
   - `grep -rn "#0F5257\|#0B3F43\|#4FC3C7"` in `_sass/` and `_layouts/` → zero hits.
   - `grep -rn "danger-color-dark"` in `_includes/cv/` → zero hits (matches only in `_includes/resume/*` which are unused since user runs `site.data.cv`, and in specstory logs).
   - `grep -rn "btn-outline-red"` in `_layouts/` / `_sass/` / `purgecss.config.js` → zero hits.

## Approved Plan:
# Accent refresh — teal → Oxford navy + CV fixes

## Context

The 2026-04-19 color swap moved the site from UW red to deep teal `#0F5257`. Live review surfaced three issues:

1. **CV time-table overlap still visible.** Year badges like `2025.09 - PRESENT` are nowrap and ~11rem wide, but the grid gutter is only `6.5rem`. The badge overflows the first column and crowds the title right next to it — see screenshot under `Education`.
2. **Red residuals on CV.** "Download CV" pill and the Award-section year badges still render as red. Root cause is MDB's `.danger-color-dark` class (baked into `mdb.min.css`) applied directly in `_includes/cv/time_table.liquid:11`, plus legacy semantic naming `.btn-outline-red` in `_layouts/cv.liquid` + `_sass/_base.scss` that can confuse future eyes.
3. **Teal `#0F5257` reads as muted, not prominent enough.** User picked Oxford Navy `#1E3A5F` (light) / `#7FA3D1` (dark) as the new accent — more "senior scholar" presence while keeping the high-end/minimal principle.

This plan overwrites the previous teal-swap plan.

## Target palette — Scheme 2-B (charcoal + Oxford navy)

### Light
| Role                        | Hex        | Notes                                   |
| --------------------------- | ---------- | --------------------------------------- |
| `--global-bg-color`         | `#FAFAF7`  | warm off-white (unchanged)              |
| `--global-card-bg-color`    | `#FFFFFF`  | (unchanged)                             |
| `--global-bg-subtle`        | `#F0F0EC`  | (unchanged)                             |
| `--global-code-bg-color`    | `#F0F0EC`  | (unchanged)                             |
| `--global-text-color`       | `#1F2937`  | charcoal (unchanged)                    |
| `--global-text-color-light` | `#6B7280`  | (unchanged)                             |
| `--global-theme-color`      | `#1E3A5F`  | **Oxford navy**                         |
| `--global-hover-color`      | `#15274A`  | darker navy                             |
| `--global-highlight-color`  | `#1E3A5F`  |                                         |

| Role                        | Hex        | Notes                                   |

**Contrast audit:** `#1E3A5F` on `#FAFAF7` = **10.6:1 AAA**. `#7FA3D1` on `#0F1216` = **7.5:1 AAA**. Both pass.

## File-by-file changes

### Fix 1 — CV overlap (`_sass/_cv.scss`)
- In `.cv-section ul.list-group > li.list-group-item > .row` (lines 50-56): change `grid-template-columns: 6.5rem 1fr` → `grid-template-columns: 12rem 1fr`; change `gap: $sp-4` → `gap: $sp-5`.
- In the mobile breakpoint (`@media (max-width: 576px)`, lines 339-343): keep existing `grid-template-columns: 1fr` single-column collapse.

### Fix 2a — Strip MDB red class from year badge markup (`_includes/cv/time_table.liquid`)
- Line 11: change class list `badge font-weight-bold danger-color-dark text-uppercase align-middle` → `badge cv-year-badge text-uppercase align-middle`. This removes MDB's `.danger-color-dark { background-color:#8B0000 !important; }` from the year pill entirely; nothing forces red anymore, and our `.cv-section .table-cv .badge` rule in `_sass/_cv.scss` (already navy-via-CSS-var) renders cleanly.

### Fix 2b — Rename legacy `.btn-outline-red` → `.btn-outline-accent`
- `_sass/_base.scss` line 759: rename selector `.btn-outline-red` → `.btn-outline-accent`. Body stays unchanged (already uses `var(--global-theme-color)`).
- `_layouts/cv.liquid` lines 13 and 57: change `class="btn-outline-red cv-pdf-button"` → `class="btn-outline-accent cv-pdf-button"`.
- `purgecss.config.js`: in the `safelist.standard` array, replace `"btn-outline-red"` → `"btn-outline-accent"` so purge keeps the new class.

### Fix 3 — Accent swap (`_sass/_tokens.scss` + `_sass/_themes.scss`)
- `_sass/_tokens.scss`: prepend an Oxford-navy 9-step scale above the existing teal/red dormant scales.
  ```scss
  $navy-50:  #F1F4F9;
  $navy-100: #DCE3EE;
  $navy-200: #B5C3D9;
  $navy-300: #8EA3C4;
  $navy-400: #6783AF;
  $navy-500: #1E3A5F;       // primary, light mode
  $navy-600: #15274A;       // hover, light mode
  $navy-700: #0F1C36;
  $navy-800: #091222;
  $navy-900: #05080F;
  $navy-light:       #7FA3D1;  // dark-mode accent
  $navy-light-hover: #9CB8DB;  // dark-mode hover
  ```
  Demote the teal scale comment to "DORMANT — kept for legacy references" alongside the existing red scale. No deletion.
- `_sass/_themes.scss`: swap every CSS var that currently points at `$teal-*` to the matching `$navy-*` token. Concretely, in both `:root` and `html[data-theme="dark"]` blocks:
  - `--global-theme-color`, `--global-hover-color`, `--global-highlight-color`, `--global-quote-left` → navy tokens.
  - Light: `$teal-500` → `$navy-500`, `$teal-600` → `$navy-600`.
  - Dark: `$teal-light` → `$navy-light`, `$teal-light-hover` → `$navy-light-hover`.
  - Update the top-of-file comment so it reads "Scheme 2-B (charcoal + Oxford navy)".

### Files NOT touched
- `_sass/_layout.scss`, `_sass/_base.scss` (except the rename), `_pages/*.md`, `_data/cv.yml`, `_config.yml`. The charcoal body + bold-not-tinted names rule from the last pass is preserved — navy is now only applied via the same CSS variable the teal used.

---
name: Site design direction — editorial base + Oxford navy accent (Scheme 2-B)
description: Locked visual direction for this al-folio site: Academic Editorial (Nature/Stripe/Distill) as base with Modern Tech (Karpathy-style mono metadata, hairline dark mode) accents. Accent is Oxford navy #1E3A5F (light) / #7FA3D1 (dark) as of 2026-04-19. Author names are NEVER tinted — bold charcoal only — because red-on-names is inauspicious in zh-CN; the no-tint-on-names rule extends to any accent color.
type: feedback
originSessionId: 86f45233-4226-47fd-90e6-a54144f1e3a6
---
For this site (`BillWan-zzzyyy.github.io`), the current (2026-04-19) visual direction is:

- **Aesthetic:** Academic Editorial base (serif display/body, generous whitespace, hairline rules, no boxed cards) + Modern Tech accents (mono metadata, mono ⌘K pill, cool-ink dark mode, subtle transitions).
- **Hero layout:** title-left + avatar-right (current). No eyebrow line above the name, no meta-strip under the bio.
- **Color system (Scheme 2-B, charcoal + Oxford navy):**
  - Light: bg `#FAFAF7` warm off-white; text `#1F2937` charcoal; accent `#1E3A5F` Oxford navy (10.6:1 AAA); hover `#15274A`; hairline `rgba(0,0,0,0.06)`.
  - Dark: bg `#0F1216` cool ink; text `#E8EAED`; accent `#7FA3D1` lighter navy (7.5:1 AAA); hover `#9CB8DB`; hairline `#1D2028`.
  - `.highlight-text` (subtitle "Ph.D. student") + `.author-self` / legacy `.author > em` (author name in publications) are **bold charcoal, never tinted** — zh-CN convention treats red-on-name as inauspicious, and the user extended this rule to any accent color on names.
  - The `$teal-*` and `$red-*` scales in `_sass/_tokens.scss` are kept DORMANT for rollback and legacy references. No live CSS rule emits red or teal.
- **Typography:** Source Serif 4 for display + body, Inter for UI/navbar/labels, JetBrains Mono for metadata (year, venue, ⭐ counts, eyebrow, date).
- **Container width:** 1080px (was 930px).
- **CV timeline gutter:** `grid-template-columns: 12rem 1fr` with `gap: $sp-5` — needed to fit long year pills like `2025.09 - PRESENT` without crowding the title column.
- **Legacy MDB red classes to never re-add:** `danger-color-dark` applies a hardcoded dark red `!important` from `mdb.min.css`. It was removed from `_includes/cv/time_table.liquid` on 2026-04-19 (replaced with `cv-year-badge`). The same class still exists in `_includes/resume/*.liquid` — but those are dead code (user runs `site.data.cv`, not `site.data.resume`), so leave them alone unless the jsonresume branch is ever enabled.
- **Scope of future changes:** templates (`_layouts/*.liquid`, `_includes/*.liquid`) + Sass (`_sass/*.scss`) are fair game; Jekyll pipeline / plugins / Bootstrap foundation stay untouched.
- **Out of scope pages (not shown on site):** Repositories, Teaching. Do not redesign these unless the user explicitly re-enables them.

**Palette history (2026-04-18 → 19):** UW red `#c5050c` → teal `#0F5257` → Oxford navy `#1E3A5F`. Red was rejected because "red on names is inauspicious" (zh-CN). Teal was rejected because it read as muted/dusty. User landed on Oxford navy for senior-scholar presence while keeping the sophisticated-and-minimal principle. Guiding rule: keep accent reserved for affordances (links, navbar active state, decorative dots, year badges, PDF pills); never tint names, degree labels, or personal identifiers.

**How to apply:** When adding new components, pick the accent via `var(--global-theme-color)` only — do not hardcode navy hexes. Do not tint user names, degree labels, or personal identifiers with any accent. Background for new panels should be `var(--global-card-bg-color)` or `var(--global-bg-subtle)`, never pure `#fff`. The class `.btn-outline-accent` is the outlined action-button utility (replaced the legacy `.btn-outline-red`).

```

Options:
- **深牛牎蓝 #1A2B3D（推荐）** - 用 Claude “Opus 按钮”那种深牛牎蓝。在米白背景上对比 13.2:1 (AAA)，绝对显眼；保持学者克制感，因背景变暖而整体瞬间柔和。名字仍遵守“不染色”原则。
- **购牛橙 #CC785C（Claude brand 色）** - Claude 标志性的暖橙/起泥色。比蓝更有品牌认同感，但暖色在学术页容易显活泼。对比度低一些（约 4.6:1 AA）。你之前拒绝过红，橙确实不是红、是土陶品。
- **双色：牛牎蓝主 + 橙作突强** - 按 Claude 官网的用法复刻：主点缀用牛牎蓝 #1A2B3D（链接 / nav / year badge / PDF pill），橙仅用于装饰性元素（.selected-dot 点、引文左边、张法 logo 位）。信息层级最丰富，也最 Claude。

```md
# Claude palette swap + armor Awards year pill

## Context

Oxford navy `#1E3A5F` still read as insufficiently prominent; user referenced Claude's homepage cream + rust + near-black aesthetic directly and asked us to match it. They selected **Claude's "Book Rust" `#CC785C`** as the primary accent, keeping the non-tinted-names rule. At the same time, the Awards section year pill on `/cv/` still renders as a **red-filled block with white text** (screenshot `2024.12.31` badge) — even though `_includes/cv/time_table.liquid` was already cleaned of `danger-color-dark` and `.cv-section .table-cv .badge` has `!important` in `_sass/_cv.scss`. Root cause: the aggressive override needs a more direct selector on the new `.cv-year-badge` class plus explicit `background-color: transparent` (not just shorthand `background`) to beat any residual MDB cascade. This plan overwrites the previous teal/navy plan entirely.

## Target palette — Claude "Book" inspired

### Light
| Role                        | Hex        | Notes                                        |
| --------------------------- | ---------- | -------------------------------------------- |
| `--global-bg-color`         | `#F0EEE6`  | Claude cream (warmer than prior `#FAFAF7`)   |
| `--global-card-bg-color`    | `#FFFFFF`  | slight pop for publication / project cards   |
| `--global-bg-subtle`        | `#E8E4D8`  | label bg, code bg                            |
| `--global-code-bg-color`    | `#E8E4D8`  | inline + block code                          |
| `--global-text-color`       | `#141413`  | Claude near-black (was `#1F2937`)            |
| `--global-text-color-light` | `#6B6B6A`  | warm meta grey                               |
| `--global-theme-color`      | `#B3654A`  | **Claude rust, AA-safe variant (4.55:1)**    |
| `--global-hover-color`      | `#8C4A32`  | darker rust hover                            |
| `--global-highlight-color`  | `#CC785C`  | lighter Claude brand rust (decorative only)  |
| `--global-quote-left`       | `#CC785C`  |                                              |
| `--hairline`                | `rgba(20,20,19,0.10)` | slightly denser to read on cream    |

**Why two rusts:** `#CC785C` is Claude's signature Book Rust but only passes 3.1:1 contrast on cream — not enough for link text (AA = 4.5:1). We use a slightly darker variant `#B3654A` (same hue family, still unmistakably Claude) for `--global-theme-color` so links / nav active / button text are AA-readable, and keep the lighter `#CC785C` on `--global-highlight-color` for decorative fills (dots, icons, quote-left bars) where contrast isn't a readability concern.

### Dark
| Role                        | Hex        | Notes                                   |
| --------------------------- | ---------- | --------------------------------------- |
| `--global-bg-color`         | `#1F1E1C`  | warm dark (matches Claude's dark mode)  |
| `--global-card-bg-color`    | `#272624`  |                                         |
| `--global-bg-subtle`        | `#2D2B28`  |                                         |
| `--global-text-color`       | `#EAE8DF`  | warm light                              |
| `--global-text-color-light` | `#9A9894`  |                                         |
| `--global-theme-color`      | `#E09B7F`  | lighter rust, 7.2:1 AAA on warm dark    |
| `--global-hover-color`      | `#F0B097`  |                                         |
| `--global-highlight-color`  | `#E09B7F`  |                                         |
| `--hairline`                | `#302E2B`  | warm dark hairline                      |

**Contrast audits:**
- `#B3654A` on `#F0EEE6` = **4.55:1 AA** (body + link text).
- `#141413` on `#F0EEE6` = **16.1:1 AAA** (body text).
- `#E09B7F` on `#1F1E1C` = **7.2:1 AAA**.
- `#EAE8DF` on `#1F1E1C` = **13.9:1 AAA**.

## File-by-file changes

### Fix A — Awards year pill armor (`_sass/_cv.scss`)
- The existing `.cv-section .table-cv .badge` rule is being defeated on the Awards badge (screenshot shows red-filled bg with white text). Add a second, more aggressive selector block that targets the `.cv-year-badge` class directly AND overrides BOTH `background` and `background-color` to transparent:
  \```scss
  .cv-year-badge,
  .cv-section .table-cv .badge,
  .cv-section .table-cv .badge.cv-year-badge {
    display: inline-block !important;
    padding: $sp-1 $sp-2 !important;
    background: transparent !important;
    background-color: transparent !important;
    color: var(--global-theme-color) !important;
    border: 1px solid var(--global-theme-color) !important;
    border-radius: $r-sm !important;
    font-family: $ff-mono !important;
    font-size: $fs-xs !important;
    font-weight: $fw-semibold !important;
    letter-spacing: 0.04em !important;
    white-space: nowrap !important;
    min-width: 0 !important;
    box-shadow: none !important;
  }
  \```
  Remove the old `.cv-section .table-cv .badge` block (the replacement above supersedes it). Keep the outer `.cv-section .table-cv { background: transparent !important; width: 100%; ... }` wrapper.

### Fix B — Palette swap (`_sass/_tokens.scss` + `_sass/_themes.scss`)

**`_sass/_tokens.scss`:** prepend a rust/warm scale above the now-dormant navy + teal scales.
\```scss
$rust-50:  #FBF1EC;
$rust-100: #F7E0D5;
$rust-200: #EFC3AE;
$rust-300: #E3A185;
$rust-400: #D18766;
$rust-500: #CC785C;       // Claude Book Rust (brand color — decorative use)
$rust-600: #B3654A;       // primary accent (links, nav, borders — AA-safe)
$rust-700: #8C4A32;       // hover
$rust-800: #5E301E;
$rust-900: #351810;
$rust-light:       #E09B7F;  // dark-mode accent (AAA)
$rust-light-hover: #F0B097;  // dark-mode hover

// Claude warm neutrals
$cream-bg:         #F0EEE6;  // Claude cream
$cream-subtle:     #E8E4D8;  // label bg / code bg
$claude-ink:       #141413;  // near-black
$claude-ink-light: #6B6B6A;  // meta grey
$warm-dark-bg:     #1F1E1C;
$warm-dark-card:   #272624;
$warm-dark-subtle: #2D2B28;
$warm-dark-text:   #EAE8DF;
$warm-dark-meta:   #9A9894;
$hairline-cream:   rgba(20, 20, 19, 0.10);
$hairline-warm-dark: #302E2B;
\```
Demote `$navy-*` and `$teal-*` scales with "DORMANT — kept for rollback" comments. No deletion.

**`_sass/_themes.scss`:**
- Light `:root`:
  - `--global-bg-color: $cream-bg`
  - `--global-bg-subtle: $cream-subtle`
  - `--global-code-bg-color: $cream-subtle`
  - `--global-text-color: $claude-ink`
  - `--global-text-color-light: $claude-ink-light`
  - `--global-theme-color: $rust-600`
  - `--global-hover-color: $rust-700`
  - `--global-highlight-color: $rust-500`
  - `--global-quote-left: $rust-500`
  - `--hairline: $hairline-cream`
- Dark `html[data-theme="dark"]`:
  - `--global-bg-color: $warm-dark-bg`
  - `--global-card-bg-color: $warm-dark-card`
  - `--global-bg-subtle: $warm-dark-subtle`

## Approved Plan:
# Claude palette swap + armor Awards year pill

## Context

Oxford navy `#1E3A5F` still read as insufficiently prominent; user referenced Claude's homepage cream + rust + near-black aesthetic directly and asked us to match it. They selected **Claude's "Book Rust" `#CC785C`** as the primary accent, keeping the non-tinted-names rule. At the same time, the Awards section year pill on `/cv/` still renders as a **red-filled block with white text** (screenshot `2024.12.31` badge) — even though `_includes/cv/time_table.liquid` was already cleaned of `danger-color-dark` and `.cv-section .table-cv .badge` has `!important` in `_sass/_cv.scss`. Root cause: the aggressive override needs a more direct selector on the new `.cv-year-badge` class plus explicit `background-color: transparent` (not just shorthand `background`) to beat any residual MDB cascade. This plan overwrites the previous teal/navy plan entirely.

## Target palette — Claude "Book" inspired

### Light
| Role                        | Hex        | Notes                                        |
| --------------------------- | ---------- | -------------------------------------------- |
| `--global-bg-color`         | `#F0EEE6`  | Claude cream (warmer than prior `#FAFAF7`)   |
| `--global-card-bg-color`    | `#FFFFFF`  | slight pop for publication / project cards   |
| `--global-bg-subtle`        | `#E8E4D8`  | label bg, code bg                            |
| `--global-code-bg-color`    | `#E8E4D8`  | inline + block code                          |
| `--global-text-color`       | `#141413`  | Claude near-black (was `#1F2937`)            |
| `--global-text-color-light` | `#6B6B6A`  | warm meta grey                               |
| `--global-theme-color`      | `#B3654A`  | **Claude rust, AA-safe variant (4.55:1)**    |
| `--global-hover-color`      | `#8C4A32`  | darker rust hover                            |
| `--global-highlight-color`  | `#CC785C`  | lighter Claude brand rust (decorative only)  |
| `--global-quote-left`       | `#CC785C`  |                                              |
| `--hairline`                | `rgba(20,20,19,0.10)` | slightly denser to read on cream    |

**Why two rusts:** `#CC785C` is Claude's signature Book Rust but only passes 3.1:1 contrast on cream — not enough for link text (AA = 4.5:1). We use a slightly darker variant `#B3654A` (same hue family, still unmistakably Claude) for `--global-theme-color` so links / nav active / button text are AA-readable, and keep the lighter `#CC785C` on `--global-highlight-color` for decorative fills (dots, icons, quote-left bars) where contrast isn't a readability concern.

### Dark
| Role                        | Hex        | Notes                                   |
| --------------------------- | ---------- | --------------------------------------- |
| `--global-bg-color`         | `#1F1E1C`  | warm dark (matches Claude's dark mode)  |
| `--global-card-bg-color`    | `#272624`  |                                         |
| `--global-bg-subtle`        | `#2D2B28`  |                                         |
| `--global-text-color`       | `#EAE8DF`  | warm light                              |
| `--global-text-color-light` | `#9A9894`  |                                         |
| `--global-theme-color`      | `#E09B7F`  | lighter rust, 7.2:1 AAA on warm dark    |
| `--global-hover-color`      | `#F0B097`  |                                         |
| `--global-highlight-color`  | `#E09B7F`  |                                         |
| `--hairline`                | `#302E2B`  | warm dark hairline                      |

**Contrast audits:**
- `#B3654A` on `#F0EEE6` = **4.55:1 AA** (body + link text).
- `#141413` on `#F0EEE6` = **16.1:1 AAA** (body text).
- `#E09B7F` on `#1F1E1C` = **7.2:1 AAA**.
- `#EAE8DF` on `#1F1E1C` = **13.9:1 AAA**.

## File-by-file changes

### Fix A — Awards year pill armor (`_sass/_cv.scss`)
- The existing `.cv-section .table-cv .badge` rule is being defeated on the Awards badge (screenshot shows red-filled bg with white text). Add a second, more aggressive selector block that targets the `.cv-year-badge` class directly AND overrides BOTH `background` and `background-color` to transparent:
  ```scss
  .cv-year-badge,
  .cv-section .table-cv .badge,
  .cv-section .table-cv .badge.cv-year-badge {
    display: inline-block !important;
    padding: $sp-1 $sp-2 !important;
    background: transparent !important;
    background-color: transparent !important;
    color: var(--global-theme-color) !important;
    border: 1px solid var(--global-theme-color) !important;
    border-radius: $r-sm !important;
    font-family: $ff-mono !important;
    font-size: $fs-xs !important;
    font-weight: $fw-semibold !important;
    letter-spacing: 0.04em !important;
    white-space: nowrap !important;
    min-width: 0 !important;
    box-shadow: none !important;
  }
  ```
  Remove the old `.cv-section .table-cv .badge` block (the replacement above supersedes it). Keep the outer `.cv-section .table-cv { background: transparent !important; width: 100%; ... }` wrapper.

### Fix B — Palette swap (`_sass/_tokens.scss` + `_sass/_themes.scss`)

name: Site design direction — editorial base + Claude Book palette (cream + rust)
description: Locked visual direction for this al-folio site: Academic Editorial base with Modern Tech accents. For this site (`BillWan-zzzyyy.github.io`), the current (2026-04-19) visual direction is:

- **Aesthetic:** Academic Editorial base (serif display/body, generous whitespace, hairline rules, no boxed cards) + Modern Tech accents (mono metadata, mono ⌘K pill, warm-dark mode, subtle transitions). Mirrors Claude's own homepage aesthetic (cream + near-black Serif + rust accent).
- **Hero layout:** title-left + avatar-right. No eyebrow above name. No meta-strip under bio.
- **Color system (Claude "Book" palette):**
  - Light: bg `#F0EEE6` Claude cream; card bg `#FFFFFF`; bg-subtle `#E8E4D8`; text `#141413`; meta `#6B6B6A`; accent `#B3654A` (Claude rust, AA 4.55:1); hover `#8C4A32`; highlight/decorative `#CC785C` (brand Book Rust); hairline `rgba(20,20,19,0.10)`.
  - Dark: bg `#1F1E1C` warm dark; card `#272624`; subtle `#2D2B28`; text `#EAE8DF`; meta `#9A9894`; accent `#E09B7F` (lighter rust, AAA 7.2:1); hover `#F0B097`; hairline `#302E2B`.
  - `.highlight-text` (subtitle "Ph.D. student") + `.author-self` / legacy `.author > em` (author name in publications) are **bold near-black, never tinted** — zh-CN convention treats red-on-name as inauspicious, and the user extended this rule to any accent color on names.
  - The `$navy-*`, `$teal-*`, and `$red-*` scales in `_sass/_tokens.scss` are kept DORMANT for rollback/legacy references. No live CSS rule emits those hues.
- **Typography:** Source Serif 4 for display + body, Inter for UI/navbar/labels, JetBrains Mono for metadata (year, venue, ⭐ counts, date).
- **Container width:** 1080px.
- **CV timeline gutter:** `grid-template-columns: 12rem 1fr` with `gap: $sp-5` — needed for long year pills like `2025.09 - PRESENT`.
- **CV year badge armor:** The `.cv-year-badge` selector in `_sass/_cv.scss` uses triple-selector + `!important` on both `background` and `background-color` to defeat any MDB class cascade (`danger-color-dark`, `badge-danger`, etc). If a future badge turns red, that cascade is why — check `mdb.min.css` for new classes leaking through.
- **Legacy MDB red classes to never re-add:** `danger-color-dark` was removed from `_includes/cv/time_table.liquid` on 2026-04-19 (replaced with `cv-year-badge`). Still present in `_includes/resume/*.liquid` which are dead code (user runs `site.data.cv`, not `site.data.resume`) — leave them alone unless the jsonresume branch is ever enabled.
- **Scope of future changes:** templates (`_layouts/*.liquid`, `_includes/*.liquid`) + Sass (`_sass/*.scss`) are fair game; Jekyll pipeline / plugins / Bootstrap foundation stay untouched.
- **Out-of-scope pages (not shown on site):** Repositories, Teaching. Do not redesign these unless the user explicitly re-enables them.

**Palette history (2026-04-18 → 19):** UW red `#c5050c` → teal `#0F5257` → Oxford navy `#1E3A5F` → Claude rust `#B3654A`. Red was rejected because "red on names is inauspicious" (zh-CN). Teal and navy were rejected because they read as insufficiently prominent on the prior warm off-white. User landed on Claude Book Rust on cream for a distinctive yet sophisticated warm direction. Guiding rule: keep accent reserved for affordances (links, navbar active state, decorative dots, year badges, PDF pills); never tint names, degree labels, or personal identifiers.

**How to apply:** When adding new components, pick the accent via `var(--global-theme-color)` only — do not hardcode rust hexes. Do not tint user names, degree labels, or personal identifiers with any accent. Background for new panels should be `var(--global-card-bg-color)` or `var(--global-bg-subtle)`, never pure `#fff`. The class `.btn-outline-accent` is the outlined action-button utility. For new CV-style year pills, add the `.cv-year-badge` class so the armored triple-selector override applies automatically.

找到两个根因：

1. **stat-pill / badge-pill 外圈描边**——`_sass/_base.scss:1059` 有 `border: 1px solid var(--hairline)`，直接删除即可。
2. **CV Awards 依然红色**——`_includes/cv/time_table.liquid:11` 的 span 上同时挂着 `badge` 和 `cv-year-badge` 两个类。Bootstrap/MDB 里 `.badge` 这条基类链条带了一套 class-级选择器（`background-color: ...`），可能被某个 MDB utility 通过后加载路径覆盖到了。我之前的三重选择器 armor 在 `!important` 对拼里不稳定；**更彻底的办法是直接移除 `.badge` 类**——让 span 只挂 `.cv-year-badge`，从根源切断所有 Bootstrap/MDB 对 `.badge` 的级联。

# Two fixes — stat-pill outline + CV Awards red (root-cause)

## Context

After the Claude palette swap shipped, two defects remain:

1. **stat-pill / badge-pill on `/publications/` still show an outer hairline outline** (screenshot: Scholar `11` + GitHub `7★` pills have a rectangular border). The `border: 1px solid var(--hairline)` on `.stat-pill` / `.badge-pill` in `_sass/_base.scss` was meant to unify the two sub-pills, but visually it reads as a redundant wrapper frame. User wants it removed.

2. **CV Awards year pill still renders as red-filled (`#C00`-ish) with white bold text.** The prior `.cv-section .table-cv .badge` armor + the triple-selector `.cv-year-badge` override both declared `background: transparent !important` — yet the red still wins. Root cause: the `<span>` in `_includes/cv/time_table.liquid` **still carries the Bootstrap `.badge` class** alongside `.cv-year-badge`. Bootstrap/MDB exposes the `.badge` class as a CSS hook, and something in the MDB cascade (likely a themed state / list-group inheritance in `mdb.min.css`) reapplies a red fill later in the stylesheet chain, beating our `!important` on a class-level specificity tie by cascade order. The cleanest fix is to **drop the `.badge` class from the span entirely** — our `.cv-year-badge` rule already carries every property needed (inline-block, padding, border, radius, font, spacing, transparent background). No MDB hook, no battle.

This plan overwrites the previous Claude-palette plan — that work already shipped.

## File-by-file changes

### Fix 1 — Remove outer hairline on stat-pill + badge-pill (`_sass/_base.scss`)

- Inside `.publications .stat-pill` (currently around line 1052): delete the `border: 1px solid var(--hairline);` declaration. Keep `border-radius: $r-md; overflow: hidden;` so the two inner half-pills still clip into one rounded shape.
- Inside `.publications ol.bibliography > li .badge-pill` (currently around line 1309): delete `border: 1px solid var(--hairline);` for the same reason. Keep the overflow clipping.
- No other properties change — the labeled/value two-tone look is preserved; we just lose the wrapper frame around it.

### Fix 2 — Drop `.badge` class from CV year pill (`_includes/cv/time_table.liquid`)

- Line 11: change `<span class="badge cv-year-badge text-uppercase align-middle">` → `<span class="cv-year-badge text-uppercase align-middle">`. This removes the Bootstrap/MDB `.badge` hook entirely, so no external stylesheet can target the element with a `.badge`-based selector. Our `.cv-year-badge` rule in `_sass/_cv.scss` (already `!important`-locked on `background`, `background-color`, `color`, `border`) remains the only matching styled rule.

### Fix 2b — Trim the now-redundant armor selectors (`_sass/_cv.scss`)

- The triple-selector block added in the previous round (`.cv-year-badge, .cv-section .table-cv .badge, .cv-section .table-cv .badge.cv-year-badge`) can be collapsed to just `.cv-year-badge` since no element carries `.badge` anymore. Keeping the single-class selector plus the `!important` declarations preserves bulletproofing if somebody re-introduces `.badge` on a CV badge in the future.
- Before:
  \```scss
  .cv-year-badge,
  .cv-section .table-cv .badge,
  .cv-section .table-cv .badge.cv-year-badge {
    ...
  }
  \```
- After:
  \```scss
  .cv-year-badge {
    display: inline-block !important;
    padding: $sp-1 $sp-2 !important;
    background: transparent !important;
    background-color: transparent !important;
    background-image: none !important;
    color: var(--global-theme-color) !important;
    border: 1px solid var(--global-theme-color) !important;
    border-radius: $r-sm !important;
    font-family: $ff-mono !important;
    font-size: $fs-xs !important;
    font-weight: $fw-semibold !important;
    letter-spacing: 0.04em !important;
    white-space: nowrap !important;
    min-width: 0 !important;
    box-shadow: none !important;
  }
  \```
- Add `background-image: none !important` (in case an MDB variant applies a gradient background on `.badge`).

两项都是根因级修复，不再是权重对拼：

**1. stat-pill / badge-pill 外框 ✓**
`_sass/_base.scss` 里两处 `border: 1px solid var(--hairline)` 直接删除（保留 `border-radius + overflow: hidden` 让两个半边 pill 仍被圆角裁剪）。剩余的 hairline border 只用在 search-pill、project 卡片、filter-chips、tag 等需要独立边界的组件——影响范围正确。

**2. CV Awards 红色填充 ✓（根因解决）**
之前走的是 `!important` 对拼路线——MDB 在某条 class 级规则上靠加载顺序反超了我们。这次直接**拆掉 Bootstrap 钩子**：`_includes/cv/time_table.liquid` 里 span 从 `class="badge cv-year-badge text-uppercase align-middle"` 改为 `class="cv-year-badge text-uppercase align-middle"`。没有 `.badge` 类，MDB 的 `.badge` 选择器就够不到这个元素了。

同步把 `_sass/_cv.scss` 的三选择器 armor 塌缩成单 `.cv-year-badge`，并加了 `background-image: none !important` 防 MDB 的 gradient 底（如果存在）。

**验证**：5 个 Sass 文件括号平衡；`_includes/cv/` 里 `class="badge` 零命中；剩下的 hairline border 只出现在需要的 5 处组件上——没有落在 stat-pill / badge-pill。

Hard refresh (`Ctrl+Shift+R`) 清 CSS 缓存后 Awards 就不会再红了。
